As wielders of both occult power and firearms, gunwitches pride themselves in using both unconventional weapons and obscure magic. To change their patron ([The Spinner of Threads](/Patrons.aspx?ID=16)), swap out [_nudge fate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1890) and [_sure strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709). **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Gunwitch](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3510) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 78 **Perception** +13 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +17, [Patron Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **Firearm Familiar** The gunwitch's firearm acts as their familiar but remains a mindless item with no actions. The master abilities it grants are included in the stat block. **Items** [_musket staff of force_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3587) (20 rounds) --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +15 **Will** +15 **HP** 90 **Acrobatic Dodge** **Trigger** An attacker the gunwitch can observe targets them with an attack; **Effect** The gunwitch gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack, and after the attack the gunwitch [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _musket staff_ +15 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning plus 1d6 force **Melee** fist +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning **Ranged** _musket staff_ +18 ([Concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=846), [fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 70 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+6 piercing plus 1d6 force **Occult Prepared Spells** DC 25, attack +17 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **1st** [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513) (×2), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717) (×2) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Flicker](/Spells.aspx?ID=1532), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) **Witch Hex Spells** DC 25, 1 Focus Point - **Cantrips (4th)** [Nudge Fate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1890) - **4th** [Needle of Vengeance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1881) **Bewitched Shot** **Requirements** The gunwitch is wielding their firearm familiar and has a hex bullet loaded in it (see Hex Bullet); **Effect** The gunwitch Casts a Spell that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast into their bullet, then Strikes with their firearm familiar, shooting the magic bullet. This counts as two attacks for the gunwitch's multiple attack penalty. On a hit, the target is also affected by the spell, though the target gets any normal defenses allowed by the spell.
If the spell is targeted, it targets the creature that was hit and no one else. If the spell is an area, the target must be in the area. A burst is centered on a corner of the target's square if the target is Medium or smaller or the corner of a square closest to the creature's center if it's Large or larger. A cone or line emits from a square of the gunwitch's choice adjacent to the target. **Bullet Storm** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The gunwitch is wielding their firearm familiar and has a hex bullet loaded into it (see Hex Bullet); **Effect** The gunwitch unleashes a flurry of projectiles. Each creature in a 60-foot emanation takes 8d6 piercing damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. **Hex Bullet** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The gunwitch conjures a magical hex bullet in their firearm. It can be used as a normal bullet or for the Bewitched Shot and Bullet Storm abilities. The bullet vanishes if not fired by the end of the turn. **Recall Firearm** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirement** The gunwitch's firearm familiar is within 1 mile; **Effect** The gunwitch summons their firearm into their hand or hands.
The Code Most mavericks adhere to a code of conduct, often one that's in direct opposition to the norms of society. This can make them outsiders or rebels, but it also allows them to be true to themselves and follow their own path, rather than conforming to expectations or rules imposed on them by others.
name: Gunwitch

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Those with a particular mastery over luck can make a living through games of chance as professional gamblers.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/High_Roller.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 11">[High Roller](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3511)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 79

**Perception** +22; (26 for [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) 

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +24, [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +21, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +22

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +5

**Con** +0

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +5
</row>

Gaming Arsenal The high roller treats all game tools—such as cards (used in the stat block's Strike entries), dice, coins, and gambling tokens—as melee weapons with a d4 damage die and the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602) and [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711) traits. The high roller gets a +1 item bonus to attack rolls with them and deals two damage dice. The damage type depends on the item and is determined by the GM.

**Items**
[Playing Cards](/Equipment.aspx?ID=873) (54-card deck), [_predictable silver piece_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3418), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=391)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 30 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +24 

**Will** +22 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 150

</row>

**Tip the Scales** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Trigger** A creature the high roller is observing critically fails a check; **Effect** The high roller picks up on luck that others dropped. They roll twice on their next d20 roll before the end of their next turn and take the better result.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_rapier_ +23 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d6+11 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+11 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
card +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d4+11 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
card +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 2d4+11 slashing

**Lucky Momentum** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The high roller's last action was a critical success; **Effect** The high roller either Strides twice or attempts a Strike that deals an additional 4d6 precision damage and deals half damage on a failure (but not a critical failure).

**Royal Flush Flurry** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Frequency** once per hour; **Requirements** The high roller has at least 16 cards in one hand; **Effect** The high roller unleashes the cards in a 30-foot cone, dealing 16d4 slashing damage to all creatures in the area with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. This ability expends the full deck of cards held.

</column>

Rumors circulate in the saloons that an undefeated master duelist roams the roads, waiting for their next contest. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Peerless Duelist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3512) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 80 **Perception** +24; (27 for initiative) [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +24, [Dueling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +26 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **I See You** The peerless duelist's Perception checks and firearm Strikes ignore [lesser cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) and the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition. **Items** [_obsidian goggles_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3097), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [dueling pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=520)_ ( 2, 40 rounds) --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +25 **Will** +21 **HP** 200 **Threatening Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet, Will DC 30. The duelist's presence makes foes hesitate. Any enemy that enters or starts its turn in the aura must succeed at the Will save or be [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93). Regardless of the result of its save, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 day.
**Shoot First** **Trigger** An attacker the duelist can see targets them with a Strike or spell; **Requirement** The duelist is holding a loaded firearm; **Effect** The duelist makes a firearm Strike against the triggering creature. On a critical hit, they disrupt the triggering action. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+11 bludgeoning **Ranged** _dueling pistol_ +26 ([Concealable](/Traits.aspx?ID=560), [Concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=846), [fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 3d6+13 piercing **Ace Shooter** The peerless duelist deals an extra die of damage on any [firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16) Strike they attempt. This extra damage is already included in their dueling pistol Strike. **Disarming Shot** The duelist fires a dueling pistol to attempt a [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) an enemy at range with the bullet. The duelist attempts an attack roll with the dueling pistol instead of an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check, taking any penalty appropriate for the firearm's range increment. The duelist doesn't have to meet the requirements of the Disarm action.
Instead of Disarming, the duelist can use Disarming Shot to attempt an [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) action for the benefit of themself or an ally within range. **Double Reload** **Requirements** The peerless duelist has an empty dueling pistol in each hand; **Effect** The peerless duelist Interacts to reload both dueling pistols.
name: Peerless Duelist

<title level="1" pfs="">[Bounty Hunter](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3515)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Bounty hunters are constantly on the move, whether within city walls or the wilderness, trailing their fugitive quarries for capture... or disposal. Often relying on stealth or deception as much as martial skill, bounty hunters employ a vast array of talents to accomplish their goals and collect the hefty payout.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Bounty_Hunter.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 82

**Perception** +13

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +0

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (10 bolts), [Falchion](/Weapons.aspx?ID=18), [simple manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32), [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
falchion +13 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 1d10+6 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
crossbow +14 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8+3 piercing

**Hunt Prey** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The bounty hunter designates a single creature they can see and hear, or one they're [Tracking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) or [Gathering Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391) about, as their prey. The bounty hunter gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) the prey, to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to Track the prey, and to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to Gather Information about the prey. This effect lasts until they use Hunt Prey again.

**Running Reload** <actions string="Single Action" />  The bounty hunter Stride, Steps, or [Sneaks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), and then Interacts to reload.

**Precision Edge**   The first time the bounty hunter hits their hunted prey in a round, they deal an additional 1d8 precision damage.

**Posse's Edge**   The bounty hunter and their allies gain a +1 circumstance bonus on initiative rolls if the opposing side includes their hunted prey.

</column>

markdown:
Some mercenaries eschew the complications of politics and fealty and just get paid to hunt monsters. It's a straightforward form of mercenary service, often dangerous in the extreme, but one that can yield glory and fame. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Monster Hunter](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3516) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 83 **Perception** +13 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Monster Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +11 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 **Favored Game** A monster hunter specializes in bringing down certain non-humanoid creatures. These favored game are typically [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) and [beasts](/Traits.aspx?ID=547), but an individual might hunt [dragons](/Domains.aspx?ID=106), [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), or more specialized creatures like [tigers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2867) or [manticores](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3093). **Items** [Composite Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74) (20 arrows), _+1 [greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=376)_, [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7) --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +11 **Will** +13 **HP** 105 **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _greataxe_ +15 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 1d12+8 slashing plus primal fear **Ranged** composite longbow +14 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 1d8+6 piercing plus primal fear **Hunter's Onslaught** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Requirements** The monster hunter isn't [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73); **Effect** The monster hunter leads an attack against their monstrous foe. The monster hunter chooses an enemy they can see that qualifies as their favored game. The monster hunter becomes fascinated by that creature and gains 10 temporary Hit Points that last as long as the onslaught does. During the onslaught, the hunter gains a +8 status bonus to damage rolls against the designated enemy, and allies in a 30- foot aura around the hunter gain half that bonus. The onslaught lasts for 1 minute or until either the monster hunter or the designated creature falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95). **Primal Fear** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) When the monster hunter hits a creature that qualifies as their favored game, that creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (or frightened 2 on a critical hit). **Sudden Charge** The monster hunter Strides twice and makes a melee Strike.
How To Hunt A Monster Monster hunters fight in different ways. You can replace Sudden Charge with one of the following. **Mighty Swing** The monster hunter makes a melee Strike that deals an additional 1d12 damage. This counts as two attacks when calculating their multiple attack penalty.
**Far Swing** The monster hunter makes a Strike with a melee weapon, increasing their [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) by 5 feet for that Strike.
**Brutish Maneuvers** The monster hunter can [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) an enemy up to two sizes larger than them, and can use these actions while wielding a two-handed weapon.
**Big Game Hunter** The monster hunter gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against attacks made by creatures that are Large or larger. Additionally, whenever the monster hunter deals damage to a creature that is Large or larger, they gain 5 temporary Hit Points that last until the start of their next turn.
name: Monster Hunter

Whenever high command needs an enemy spellcaster taken off the board in the midst of battle, they send in a mage killer. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Mage Killer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3517) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 84 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Items** [daggers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) (4), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=391)_, [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5) --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +17 **Will** +16 **HP** 145 **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10 **Spell Dodge** **Trigger** The mage killer is targeted by a spell; **Effect** The mage killer gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against the triggering spell.
**Spell Interception** **Trigger** A creature within 10 feet of the mage killer Casts a Spell; **Effect** The mage killer makes a melee Strike or thrown dagger Strike against the triggering creature. If it hits, the spell is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2342). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _rapier_ +20 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+10 piercing plus magical static **Melee** dagger +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+10 piercing plus magical static **Melee** fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning plus magical static **Ranged** dagger +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+10 piercing plus magical static **Magical Static** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The mage killer's Strikes deal an additional 1d8 mental damage to a creature that has Cast (or attempted to Cast) a Spell within the last round, and on a critical hit, the creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 minute. **Shift Energy Runes** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The mage killer alters the magical countermeasures in the runes on their armor. They change their resistance to the energy type of their choice (acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, sonic, vitality, or void). **Sudden Charge** The mage killer Strides twice. If they end their movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, they can make a melee Strike against it.

Some bands of experienced soldiers have storied reputations, their rolls filled with legendary warriors. Others are whispered of for their cruel deeds and opportunistic betrayals. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Mercenary Band](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3518) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 84 **Perception** +17 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Military Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +14, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +15, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +19 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** -1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +17 **Will** +15 **HP** 180 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Let 'em Have It!** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The mercenary band engages in a coordinated attack with its wide array of melee weapons against each enemy in a 5-foot emanation with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing
3d8+4 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing
4d8+6 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing **Ready… Fire!** The mercenary band draws or reloads their bows, crossbows, and slings, then launches a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a 10-foot burst within 120 feet that deals 2d8+4 piercing or bludgeoning damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. When the mercenary band is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Spoils of War** **Requirements** The band's last action was Let 'em Have It and at least one creature failed its save; **Effect** The mercenary band attempts to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) one object from each enemy that failed its save, even if the enemy is in combat or on guard.
name: Mercenary Band

Forces hire an exiled revolutionary because they were once part of the enemy. A lost scion, noble who spoke against tyranny, or wrongly persecuted politician possesses an intimate knowledge of their foe's tactics, logistics, and territory. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Exiled Revolutionary](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3519) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 85 **Perception** +17; (20 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +20, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +18 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +0 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Former Courtier** An exiled revolutionary remembers well their former realm. In their home realm, be it a manor, castle, or capital city, the exiled revolutionary gains a +4 circumstance bonus to Perception checks and Will saves, and to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40), and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, and is a 12th-level challenge in the arena of noble politics. **Items** _+1 [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=434)_ (20 arrows), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_, signet ring, [Thieves' Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758), [Wyvern Poison](/Equipment.aspx?ID=132) (2) --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +20 **Will** +17 **HP** 140 **Follow Me** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 20 feet. This aura is active only while in the exiled revolutionary's home realm, as they share knowledge to avoid guard patrols and get past checkpoints. Any ally in the aura gets a +2 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks.
**It's… You!** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) When the exiled revolutionary sees or hears someone who was part of their downfall in person, they break cover and attack their betrayer immediately, even if their actions would doom them and their allies. The revolutionary must succeed at a DC 35 Will save or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by their betrayer and unable to cease targeting them exclusively until the betrayer is defeated.
An ally can convince the revolutionary to forgo their vengeance with a DC 30 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check to make a [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393). This lasts for 1 minute, but talking the revolutionary down after that time requires more thorough engagement.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _longsword_ +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing **Melee** fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning **Ranged** _composite longbow_ +21 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 1d8+8 piercing **Darting Feint** The exiled revolutionary [Feints](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390), Steps, and Strikes in any order. **Sneak Attack** The exiled revolutionary deals an additional 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.
Betraying The Betrayed Exiled revolutionaries can make for valuable allies, especially if their goals align with the PCs. However, the exiled revolutionary has been a victim of extreme betrayal and constantly guards against it. PCs should take care when considering going back on their word with an exiled revolutionary. Should anyone act in a way that the exiled revolutionary might perceive as a betrayal, the revolutionary will dedicate their life to avenging the perceived transgression, even becoming a villainous instrument of evil should that become necessary.
name: Exiled Revolutionary

<title level="1" pfs="">[Siegebreaker](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3520)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

When a well-constructed or magically warded fortification repels every assault, a siegebreaker is called in. These masters of alchemical destruction find that the bigger and more protected the wall, the more satisfying it is to break it.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 32

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 14">[Siegebreaker](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3520)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 86

**Perception** +24

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +27, [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +29, [Siege Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +29, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +23

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +5

**Con** +4

**Int** +5

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Alchemical Grenades** A siegebreaker carries 15 alchemical grenades that deal either acid, cold, or fire damage plus 10 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and 10 [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage of the same type (typically five of each damage type). They replenish these grenades each day.

**Items**
[Alchemist's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2702), formula book, _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [light mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=360)_, _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 34 

**Fort** +25 

**Ref** +28 

**Will** +23 

Resistances [alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=528) items 10
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 300

</row>

**Explosive Compounds** When an attacker scores a critical hit against the siegebreaker, one of the siegebreaker's alchemical grenades bursts. The GM determines the grenade randomly. The siegebreaker takes damage from the grenade as though they were hit by the grenade (applying their resistance normally), and any creature in a 10-foot emanation takes the splash damage.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_light mace_ +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)),
**Damage** 2d4+18 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+18 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
alchemical grenade +27 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699)),
**Damage** 3d6 acid, cold, or fire plus 10 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and 10 [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage of the same type

**Expanded Splash**   The siegebreaker's grenades deal splash damage in a 10-foot radius.

**Quick Grenadier** <actions string="Single Action" />  The siegebreaker Interacts to draw a grenade, then Strikes with it.

**The Wall Must Fall**  ([Exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595)) **Requirements** The siegebreaker is at the base of a fortified wall; **Effect** The siegebreaker has studied for years to gain exact knowledge of how to combine the alchemical ingredients in their grenades to exponentially multiply their power, creating a terrifying siege-ender bomb that can break open a city wall. The siegebreaker spends 10 minutes combining the ingredients from 9 different alchemical grenades of their choice. The siegebreaker then sets a fuse timer up to 1 minute long. When time's up, the bomb explodes in a concentrated 20-foot burst, dealing 20d6 acid, cold, or fire damage that ignores up to 10 Hardness of structures. Any creature in the area can reduce the damage they take with a DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-542" />
name: Siegebreaker

<title level="1" pfs="">[Combat Engineer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3521)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Be it a bridge to get a battalion across a river, watchtowers to pierce the fog of war, or fortifications to secure territory, armies have always needed those who can build. The combat engineer is a soldier specializing in these sorts of constructions.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Combat Engineer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3521)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 88

**Perception** +7

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +13, [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +2

**Con** +1

**Int** +4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Logistics Specialist** In situations involving battlefield engineering or logistics, the combat engineer is a 5th-level challenge.

**Items**
entrenching tool (functions as a [pick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=389)), [Heavy Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=70) (10 bolts), [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +5 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
entrenching tool +8 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597)),
**Damage** 1d6+3 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
heavy crossbow +7 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 2](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d10 piercing

**Fortify**  ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The combat engineer digs trenches and constructs earthen barricades at a rate of one 5-foot cube per hour. A combat engineer can instead direct the work of four allied Small or larger creatures to quadruple this rate.

**Improvised Barricade** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The combat engineer has at least 5 Bulk of loose items or material within reach; **Effect** The combat engineer slaps together a 5-foot high barrier in an adjacent square. The barrier is an object with 10 Hit Points, 5 Hardness, AC 10, and it provides [standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459). After 1 minute, the barrier collapses under its own weight.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-543" />
name: Combat Engineer

markdown:
Not all soldiers are on the battlefield by choice. Often armed with modified farm implements, conscripts are usually poorly trained and poorly organized. In the heat of battle, they're prone to misunderstand or disobey orders, causing casualties on both sides. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Conscript Squad](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3523) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 89 **Perception** +8 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** -1 **Cha** +0 **Untrained Rabble** At the start of each of its turns, the conscript squad must succeed at a DC 10 Will save or be [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) that turn. --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +9 **Will** +6 –2 circumstance to all saves vs. [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **HP** 54 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Indiscriminate Assault** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The conscript squad lashes out at each other creature in a 5-foot emanation, friend and foe, with a DC 17 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8 piercing damage
1d8+4 piercing damage
2d8+4 piercing damage
name: Conscript Squad

<title level="1" pfs="">[Standard Bearer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3524)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Within a troop, the standard bearer is a beacon of morale, cohesion, and camaraderie. They proudly wave the flag of the entity they fight for. Whether they lead the spearhead or provide support from the back lines, their presence alone is usually enough to rally the soldiers around them to continue fighting.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Standard_Bearer.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Standard Bearer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3524)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 89

**Perception** +12

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +11, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +10, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +8, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Items**
battle standard (attached to ranseur), [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6), [Healer's Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25), [Ranseur](/Weapons.aspx?ID=35), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

**Inspiring Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 60 feet. The standard bearer and each ally in the aura who can see their battle standard gains a +1 status bonus to initiative rolls and saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effects. Each time an affected creature gains the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition, reduce the frightened value by 1.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ranseur +12 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d10+8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword+12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d6+8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist+12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Signal the Advance!** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The standard bearer raises their flag to the sky, signaling their allies to charge. Each ally affected by inspiring aura can use a reaction to Stand, Step, or Stride.

**Stay in the Fight!** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The standard bearer shouts an inspiring cry. Each ally affected by inspiring aura gains 10 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">The Standard's Significance</title>

To be a standard bearer is no easy task. On the battlefield, they serve as the premier representative of the organization they serve, and should their standard be captured or destroyed, it would result in a huge loss of morale. If a standard bearer returns to their company alive and without their battle standard, harsh punishments will soon follow. Outside of battle, standard bearers often make great efforts to befriend the rest of their platoon, as they're the soldier who will need the most protection once combat begins.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-543" />
name: Standard Bearer

<title level="1" pfs="">[Sniper](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3525)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A keen eye, a steady hand, and a killer instinct combine to form a ruthless, emotionless harbinger of death. A sniper usually works alone, though they're occasionally seen alongside a spotter or as part of a larger squad.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Sniper.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Sniper](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3525)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 90

**Perception** +15

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +11, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +1

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Silencer** A silencer is an uncommon item worth 1 sp. It has light Bulk and can be attached to a firearm in 1 minute; the sniper typically already has one attached before going into combat. The first time a shot is fired through it, the silencer is consumed and reduces the report to a quiet noise. A silencer doesn't work on [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) firearms.

**Items**
[Arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=195) (20 cartridges), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [Silencer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1206) (4)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 65

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S +15](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [unarmed +15](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
arquebus +15 ([range 150 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=846), [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [Kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=847), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8+6 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S +15](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 piercing

**Concussive Shot** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The sniper makes an arquebus Strike against a creature within the weapon's first range increment. On a success, the creature must succeed at a DC 21 Fortitude save or be [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) (stunned 2 on a critical failure).

**Full Bore** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The sniper makes an arquebus Strike against two creatures that are adjacent to each other. The attack ignores any [lesser cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) one target provides the other. Roll damage once, and apply it to each creature the sniper hits. This counts as two attacks when determining the sniper's multiple attack penalty.

**Sniper's Edge**   The sniper's ranged Strikes deal 2d6 extra precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

**Surprise Attack**   All enemy creatures that have not yet acted in combat are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the sniper.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Outfitting A Sniper</title>

The weapons and armor of a sniper vary depending on how they wish to get the job done. Though this one uses an [arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=518), many snipers choose to use [longbows](/Weapons.aspx?ID=436) if gunpowder isn't an option or if they prefer more subtle means of killing. Snipers usually forgo wearing the colors of their company in favor of hues that best match their surroundings. They usually only carry a small insignia for identification purposes, and their position is usually on a need-to-know basis.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-543" />
name: Sniper

Infantry are the backbone of most armies. These professional soldiers, marked by matching uniforms, straightforward tactics, and the drive to follow well-practiced orders, make up the bulk of most military forces—but are often considered the most expendable. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Line Infantry](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3526) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 90 **Perception** +13 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +14 **Will** +13 **HP** 96 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **No Retreat** These soldiers have been extensively trained to hold their ground no matter the situation. If any effect would force the line infantry to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451), reduce the distance by 5 feet. Any time they would be affected by the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) condition, the line infantry is instead [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for the same duration.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 20 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Bolt Salvo** The line infantry draws, loads, and shoots a salvo from their crossbows. The salvo is a 10-foot burst within 120 feet that deals 2d8 piercing damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). When the line infantry is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Clash of Steel** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The line infantry lays into each enemy in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+2 slashing damage
2d6+7 slashing damage
3d6+10 slashing damage **Drilled in Formations** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The line infantry uses Change Formation (see below in Military). A line infantry unit typically knows the marching column and wedge formations.
name: Line Infantry

A proper phalanx formation requires the simultaneous use of spear and shield in order to attack enemies near and far while defending each other. Impressive amounts of teamwork are not only beneficial but essential to the survival of the troop. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Phalanx Formation](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3527) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 91 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11 **Str** +5 **Dex** +0 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +12 **Will** +14 **HP** 99 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Hurl Javelins** The troop's members throw a volley of spears. Each creature in a 10-foot burst within 30 feet of the troop takes 2d6+5 piercing damage with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. When the phalanx formation is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Spears Out!** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The phalanx formation thrusts their longspears out in all directions, striking all unfortunate enough to be near them. Each enemy in a 10-foot emanation must attempt a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 piercing damage
2d8+5 piercing damage
3d8+5 piercing damage **Shields Up!** The phalanx formation raises their shields to protect one another. The formation gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and Reflex until the start of their next turn. This bonus increases to +3 against physical ranged attacks.
name: Phalanx Formation

A charging band of knights, mounted upon heavy warhorses and clad in steel plate, is a sight to be feared on the battlefield. The weight of their armor, however, makes them ill-suited to extended overland maneuvers. Squads of squires are needed to tend to horses, repair armor, and otherwise support the heavy cavalry group between battles. Despite these limitations, their ability to crash through enemy lines makes them an invaluable tool for professional armies. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Heavy Cavalry](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3528) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 92 **Perception** +14 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +12, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +13 **Will** +14 **HP** 105 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, splash damage 8 **Mounted Troop** Effects that target only [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) or only [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) might not work on the cavalry brigade, subject to the GM's discretion.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 40 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Join the Fray** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The heavy cavalry swing flails at each enemy in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+3 bludgeoning damage
2d6+7 bludgeoning damage
3d6+10 bludgeoning damage **Thunder of Hooves** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The heavy cavalry Strides. At the end of their movement, the cavalry can either attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) each adjacent enemy or an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) each enemy within 30 feet. Roll only once and compare the result to each enemy's Reflex DC (for Trip) or Will DC (for Demoralize). **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Medium or smaller, 2d8+7 bludgeoning, DC 22; creatures that fail the save are also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
name: Heavy Cavalry

markdown:
Maintaining discipline is of the utmost importance when conducting a military campaign. Often elevated from veteran soldiers, drill sergeants are responsible for training common troops, ensuring they can follow orders and fight well in the thick of battle. Though drill sergeants can be brash and hard-nosed, harsh discipline is often crucial to maintaining order and keeping soldiers alive. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Drill Sergeant](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3529) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 93 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +18 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6), [Javelin](/Weapons.aspx?ID=71) (6), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_ --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +15 **Will** +20 **HP** 120 **Commanding Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 60 feet. An ally that starts its turn in the aura gains 8 temporary Hit Points. These last until the start of the creature's next turn.
**You Don't Have My Permission to Die!** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** An allied creature within 30 feet would be reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** With a stern rebuke, the drill sergeant berates the target for their failure. The creature avoids being [knocked out](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and remains at 1 HP. The creature is then temporarily immune for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _longsword_ +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d8+12 slashing **Melee** fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+12 bludgeoning **Ranged** javelin +19 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6+12 piercing **Chastising Enforcement** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The drill sergeant exhorts a faltering comrade with a stern word and attempts an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check against the Will DC of one ally within 30 feet. On a success, the target's [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition is reduced by 2 and the drill sergeant can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) one [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect that ally is suffering from with a +18 counteract modifier. On a critical success, the drill sergeant also reduces the frightened condition of each other ally in a 10-foot emanation around the target by 1. **Keep Up With Me!** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Requirements** The drill sergeant's last action was a Strike that hit; **Effect** The drill sergeant shouts that one ally within 30 feet can't keep up with them. That ally gains a +3 status bonus to their attack roll on the next Strike they make before the start of the drill sergeant's next turn. If the ally is a [troop](/Traits.aspx?ID=367), this bonus instead applies to the DC of their next offensive activity (such as Join the Fray for [heavy cavalry](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3528)).
name: Drill Sergeant

markdown:
A Hellknight cavalry brigade consists of several Hellknights and a single field-maralictor, all wearing the distinctive armor of their order and wielding lances. The maralictor speaks for the brigade, questioning travelers the brigade encounters and barking orders. A Hellknight brigade is typically based at a keep or other fortification controlling an area measured by a day's ride in every direction—about 25 miles. Farther-ranging missions are possible but require substantial logistical support. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Hellknight Cavalry Brigade](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3530) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 93 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Hell Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +12, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +12 **Str** +7 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +16 **HP** 135 (4 segments) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 5, slashing 5 **Weaknesses** area damage 8, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 8 **Mounted Troop** Effects that target only [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) or only [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) might not work on the Hellknight cavalry brigade, subject to the GM's discretion.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 40 feet; trailblazing stride, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Arrow Volley** The Hellknights draw or reload their longbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a 10-foot burst within 100 feet that deals 3d8 piercing damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). When the troop is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Lance Charge** The brigade Strides twice with a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed. If it moves at least 10 feet, the brigade deals 3d8+14 piercing damage with a DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save to each enemy in a 10-foot emanation at the end of its movement. **Stab from the Saddle** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The brigade engages in a coordinated lance attack against each enemy in a 10-foot emanation with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+3 piercing damage
2d6+10 piercing damage
3d6+14 piercing damage **Trailblazing Stride** While moving on land, the Hellknight cavalry brigade ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).
name: Hellknight Cavalry Brigade

<title level="1" pfs="">[Mage Knight](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3531)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Though many spellcasters prefer to defend themselves with magic, some recognize that there's no substitute for a suit of steel. Mage knights defy the stereotype that spellcasters are frail, delicate, and passive and instead choose to hold their own in close-quarter combat.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 27

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Mage_Knight.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Mage Knight](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3531)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 94

**Perception** +17

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +21, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +20

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** +4

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=362)_, spellbook, [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 29 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +13 (+16 against damaging effects)

**Will** +21 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 140

</row>

**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_mace_ +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)),
**Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+11 bludgeoning

**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 28, attack +20
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
- **1st**
[Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709)
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2), [Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606)
- **3rd**
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506), [Vampiric Feast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752)
- **4th**
[Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Weapon Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758)
- **5th**
[Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Impaling Spike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1571), [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722)

**Wizard School Spells** DC 28, 2 Focus Points
- **5th**
[Energy Absorption](/Spells.aspx?ID=1897), [Force Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1896)

**Bespell Strikes** <actions string="Free Action" />  **Frequency** once per turn; **Requirements** The mage knight's most recent action was to cast a non-[cantrip](/Traits.aspx?ID=550) spell; **Effect** The mage knight siphons spell energy into one weapon they're wielding, or into one of their [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attacks. Until the end of the turn, the weapon or unarmed attack deals an extra 2d6 force damage and gains the [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) trait if it didn't have it already. If the spell dealt a different type of damage, the Strike deals this type of damage instead.

**Drain Bonded Item** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The mage knight hasn't acted yet on this turn; **Effect** The mage knight expends the power stored in their bonded item (typically their shield). This gives them the ability to cast one prepared spell they prepared today and already cast, without spending a slot.

</column>

markdown:
A harrow deck is a set of 54 cards with symbolic illustrations that serves as a sacred divining tool. Feared by the superstitious and avoided by those who know better than to tempt fate, many harrow readers live and work in traveling communities, often moving from town to town as opportunities arise. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 13 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8 [Harrow Reader](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3533) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 96 **Perception** +7 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +10, [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +5, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +5, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +5 **Str** +1 **Dex** +2 **Con** +1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Cold Reading Specialist** For encounters involving fortune-telling, the harrow reader is a 3rd-level challenge. **Items** [Harrow Deck](/Equipment.aspx?ID=824), [Sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=9) --- **AC** 14 **Fort** +3 **Ref** +6 **Will** +10 **HP** 8 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** sickle +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 1d4+1 slashing **Melee** fist +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning **Fated Doom** Though harrow readers try to avoid combat, no throw of the cards can avoid fate, so a harrow reader learns how best to survive. In the harrow reader's first encounter each day, they gain a +1 status bonus to their initiative roll, attack rolls, and AC. **Read the Harrow** ([Exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674)) The harrow reader can conduct a reading over the course of 10 minutes to duplicate the effects of the [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445) spell. The harrow reader can conduct up to five readings per day, but the flat check DC increases by 2. This is cumulative, to a maximum of DC 14 for the fifth.
Forms Of Fortune-Telling **Anthomancy**: flowers
**Astrology**: the [Cosmic Caravan](/Deities.aspx?ID=377)
**Astromancy**: movement and brightness of stars
**Chiromancy**: reading the palm's heart, life, and head lines
**Demonomancy**: asking [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387)
**Harrowing**: harrow card readings
**Haruspicy**: entrails
**Ichthyomancy**: the next fish caught
**Mazomancy**: a nursing babe
**Oneiromancy**: dreams
**Pendulum Reading**: answering yes or no questions with a pendulum
**Pyromancy**: throwing substances into fire
**Tasseomancy**: tea leaves
**Tyromancy**: coagulation of cheese
name: Harrow Reader

<title level="1" pfs="">[Coven Aspirant](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3535)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Only the foolish would bring themselves to the attention of hags, but some aspirants strive to join a hag coven out of a desire for power or companionship.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Coven_Aspirant.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Coven Aspirant](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3535)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 97

**Perception** +7

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8)

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +7, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +5, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +5, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +1

**Con** +1

**Int** +4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Items**
cauldron, [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [Dart](/Weapons.aspx?ID=68) (4)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +9 (shared confidence)

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 35

</row>

**Shared Confidence** When a coven aspirant is within 30 feet of at least two allies, they and their allies gain a +1 status bonus to Will saves.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dart +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 piercing

**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 18, attack +10
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
- **1st**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548), [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566)

**Witch Hex Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 18
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Shroud of Night](/Spells.aspx?ID=1891)
- **1st**
[Needle of Vengeance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1881)

**Forge Pact** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The coven aspirant forms a temporary [coven](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=58) with two or more willing creatures within 30 feet, all of whom must be able to cast spells. Members of the temporary coven can cast [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [_entangling flora_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515), and [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) as 2nd-rank occult innate spells at will, using DC 17 or their spellcasting DC, whichever is higher. The coven is dissolved after 3 rounds or when all but one member is dead, whichever comes first. A creature can be a member of only one temporary coven at a time and can join a temporary coven no more than once per 24 hours.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Unusual Covens</title>

Though rare, [covens](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=58) can form that include non-[hags](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=425), as long as at least two hags are a part of the coven. [Changeling](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=67), vengeful nature spirits, intelligent [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), and [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) sometimes ally themselves with hags. The unusual composition of the coven alters the spells granted by the partnership, typically replacing the [_cursed metamorphosis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479) spell with a spell appropriate to the coven's new member.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-544" />
name: Coven Aspirant

<title level="1" pfs="">[Enigmatic Conspiracist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3536)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Powerful organizations work out of public view, shaping lives while facing few consequences. Searching for these secret societies, whether to join them or destroy them, has given the enigmatic conspiracist uncanny insight.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Enigmatic_Conspiracist.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Enigmatic Conspiracist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3536)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 98

**Perception** +10; (12 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) 

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +9, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +11, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +12, [Secret Society Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +4

**Con** +0

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Items**
_[everlight crystal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3021)_, [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36), [Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=77) (20 arrows)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

**Resistances**
[mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 5

**Knowing Glance** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** The enigmatic conspiracist is targeted by a melee Strike or touch spell; **Effect** With an uncanny look, the enigmatic conspiracist [Demoralizes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) the creature that targeted them. Demoralize loses the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait and gains the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait, and the conspiracist doesn't take a penalty if the creature doesn't understand their language. If the [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check critically succeeds, the conspiracist [disrupts](/Rules.aspx?ID=2342) the triggering action.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rapier +14 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d8+6 piercing plus spill secrets

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortbow +14 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing plus spill secrets

**Spill Secrets**  ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When the conspiracist critically hits with a Strike, the target must succeed at a DC 21 Will save or the enigmatic conspiracist perceives the target's surface thoughts for 1 round, as [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602). This grants the conspiracist a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against any creature whose mind they're reading.

**Unbelievable Connection** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The enigmatic conspiracist recites a convoluted conspiracy theory about a creature within 30 feet, then attempts an [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) check against the Will DC of that creature. On a success, the target is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 minute and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the conspiracist's attacks until no longer stupefied.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Mystic Organizations</title>

Golarion has numerous secretive societies. The **Church of Razmir** offers a plan of 31 steps to divinity. The **Esoteric Order of the Palatine Eye** seeks celestial truths said to be granted by an ancient angel. The **Knights of the Aeon Star** search for secret lore. Followers of **Rivethun**, dwarven animism, reach out to spirits to gain knowledge and earn favors.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-544" />
name: Enigmatic Conspiracist
