<title level="1" pfs="">[False Priest](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3537)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Belief is perhaps the strongest force in the universe. Instilling belief only to use it against someone in deceit, however, is the purview of a false priest.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[False Priest](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3537)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 98

**Perception** +10

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +12, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +12, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +8, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Items**
[alchemical tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=59) (used as “blessed items” to fool marks), [Backpack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4), cloak, collection of fake relics, [Hand Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69) (20 bolts), [Rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36), [wooden religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745), [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +11 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +10 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 50

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rapier +12 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6+8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hand crossbow +12 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 piercing

**Deceiver's Surprise**   On the first round of combat, if the false priest rolls [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) for initiative, creatures that haven't acted yet are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to them.

**Fickle Prophecy** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The false priest convinces another creature of their omnipotence by attempting a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check compared to the creature's Will DC. If successful, the target gains 1d8+4 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 hour or until the false priest removes them by rebuking the target, whichever occurs first.

**The Jig Is Up** <actions string="Reaction" />  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** The false priest critically fails a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check; **Effect** The false priest Strides.

**Sneak Attack**   The false priest deals an additional 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. This increases to 2d6 against creatures off-guard due to the false priest's [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390) or deceiver's surprise.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-544" />
name: False Priest

<title level="1" pfs="">[Necromancer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3538)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Defiling the natural order and spitting in the face of convention, the necromancer remains dutifully committed to understanding what forces await beyond the mortal boundaries of life and death.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Necromancer.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Necromancer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3538)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 99

**Perception** +10

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +8, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** +4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** -1
</row>

**Stench of Decay** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) The necromancer emits a scent of putrid rot in a 5-foot emanation. A living creature that enters or begins its turn in the aura is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91).

**Items**
hooded robe, [Light Mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +11 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 65

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
light mace +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 23
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)

**Undead, Arise!** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The necromancer summons two Medium undead entities in different empty squares up to 30 feet away. These undead entities can take the form of zombies, skeletons, or ghosts, chosen by the necromancer. The entities block movement as though they were creatures and can be attacked. Each entity has 1 Hit Point and the same AC and saves as the necromancer. They can't take actions of their own and deteriorate if the necromancer is reduced to 0 Hit Points. The necromancer can have up to four undead entities at any given time. If they call another, the oldest undead entity deteriorates.

**Undead, Attack!** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The necromancer has at least one undead entity active; **Effect** The necromancer commands all their undead entities to attack. Each entity can Stride up to 20 feet into an empty square and make a Strike. The Strike has a +15 attack modifier and deals 2d12 bludgeoning damage (or spirit damage if the entity is a spirit). The Strike has the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) trait, and no multiple attack penalty applies to it.

**Wave of Death** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Requirements** The necromancer isn't [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) and has at least one undead entity active; **Effect** The necromancer overloads their undead entities with void energy, causing all of them to explode. Each entity is destroyed, dealing 4d12 void damage to each creature in a 10-foot emanation with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature in more than one explosion is damaged only once. The necromancer becomes drained 1.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-544" />
name: Necromancer

A career of mystical accomplishments combined with a lifetime of subterfuge and intimidation has elevated this occultist to a powerful position. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Cult Leader](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3539) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 100 **Perception** +14 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +13, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +13 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Items** ceremonial robes, indecipherable book of sigils ([spellbook](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2753)), _+1 [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398)_ --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +15 **Will** +18 **HP** 95 **Protect the Master!** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Trigger** The cult leader is targeted with an attack, and a lower-ranking cultist is adjacent to them; **Effect** The cult leader orders their cultist to leap in front of the attack. The cultist and cult leader swap places, and the cultist becomes the target of the attack. If the cultist has Fanatical Frenzy or a similar ability, they can activate it as a reaction if they take damage from the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _shortsword_ +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+6 piercing plus 2d8 void **Melee** fist +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 26, attack +18 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **1st** [Bless](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [Lock](/Spells.aspx?ID=1589), [Phantasmal Minion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [Calm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [Laughing Fit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583), [Stupefy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (4 slots) - **4th** [Honeyed Words](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (3 slots) **Gather Converts** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) With a short emotional phrase, the cult leader tries to sway the public to do their bidding. The cult leader tries to convince up to four bystanders in a crowd to cause a commotion, turn against a person or group, leave the area, protect the cult leader, or calm down. The cult leader attempts a single [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check against the highest Perception DC among the targets.
**Critical Success** The targets believe the lie and act as directed for 1 minute. Additionally, one bystander remains by the cult leader's side, influenced enough to join the cult. All other targets become wise to the cult leader after 1 minute, at which point their attitude toward the leader worsens by one step.
**Success** As a critical success, but no bystander joins the cult permanently.
**Critical Failure** The crowd is unmoved and unamused, and their attitude toward the cult leader worsens by one step.
name: Cult Leader

<title level="1" pfs="">[Demonologist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3540)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Demonologists can pull a creature from the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45) and bend it to their will... for a time.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Demonologist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3540)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 100

**Perception** +15

**Languages**
[Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Academia Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +16, [Demon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** +4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Demonic Temptation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) Demonic study has garnered the attention of at least one [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) who is actively trying to possess the demonologist. When the demonologist publicly espouses the benefits of demonic power (whether they believe it a good thing or not), they gain a +1 status bonus to skill checks, AC, and saves for 1 day. These bonuses don't apply against [demons](/Traits.aspx?ID=573). At the end of the day, the demonologist must attempt a DC 20 Will save, becoming possessed for 1 day on a failure (or permanently on a critical failure).

**Items**
_+1 [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=361)_, robes, _Fiendish Hypotheses and Protections from Same_ ([spellbook](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2753))

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 22 

**Fort** +13 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 100

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_longspear_ +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d8+9 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+9 bludgeoning

**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 25, attack +17
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Caustic Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1461), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646)
- **1st**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (×2), [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597)
- **2nd**
[Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [Environmental Endurance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1517), [Laughing Fit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)
- **3rd**
[Acid Grip](/Spells.aspx?ID=1436), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=1547), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677)
- **4th**
[Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [Dispelling Globe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748)

**Breach the Outer Rifts** <actions string="Free Action" />  **Requirements** The demonologist's last action was to cast a non-[cantrip](/Traits.aspx?ID=550) spell; **Effect** The demonologist siphons energy drawn from the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45) into their weapon. Until the end of the turn, the weapon deals an extra 2d6 damage. Roll 1d20 to determine the type: 1–7 acid, 8–9 cold, 10–11 electricity, 12–18 fire, 19–20 void.

**Demon Summoning**   The demonologist can cast a 5th-rank [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1701) arcane spell to summon a demon. To do so, they must sacrifice two 4th-rank prepared spells and voluntarily take 4d12 mental damage that can't be reduced or prevented. If the demonologist is unable to Sustain the Spell, including if they're knocked out or killed, the spell continues, but the GM rolls a DC 10 flat check each round, ending the spell on a failure.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-544" />
name: Demonologist

markdown:
Seeking to be the most powerful and perfect creature in their domain, a mirror seer forges a deal with a nefarious entity for more power. Through a magic mirror called a [_malefic mirror_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3590), they communicate with this entity and spy on the events that unfold in their realm. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Mirror Seer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3541) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 101 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +19, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17 **Str** +2 **Dex** +2 **Con** -1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Looking Glass Magic** The mirror seer accesses power from their wicked
benefactor through two mirrors: one full-sized [_malefic mirror_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3590) in their sanctum and an _enchanted hand mirror_ they can carry on their person.
  • **Malefic Mirror** The mirror seer must visit the _malefic mirror_ once per day to retain their spellcasting abilities, and they can activate the mirror for special [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662) and [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) spells as noted in the mirror's stat block.
  • **Enchanted Hand Mirror** Without their enchanted hand mirror on their person, the mirror seer takes a –2 circumstance penalty to spell attack rolls and DCs and can't cast their 7th-rank spells. If it's not attended by the mirror seer, the hand mirror has AC 10, Hardness 0, and 1 HP.
**Items** [_+1 dagger_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [_enchanted hand mirror_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=35), [_scroll of false vision_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1522), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)_ --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +17 **Will** +20 **HP** 140 **Rightfully Mine** **Trigger** The mirror seer observes a creature making a Strike, casting a spell of 4th rank or lower, or using a special action (the triggering action must take 2 actions or fewer); Effect The mirror seer expends a 4th-rank spell slot (or higher) to duplicate the triggering action. This mimicked action occurs immediately after the triggering action, using the triggering creature's statistics unless the mirror seer's are higher. The creature the mirror seer mimicked is then temporarily immune to this ability for 10 minutes. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _staff_ +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 2d4+8 bludgeoning **Melee** dagger +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+8 piercing **Melee** fist +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** dagger +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+8 piercing **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **1st** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1439), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579) (3 slots) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653), [Status](/Spells.aspx?ID=1690) (3 slots) - **3rd** [Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [Hypnotize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588) (3 slots) - **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [Detect Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1487), [Peaceful Bubble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1624) (3 slots) - **5th** [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1666) (2 slots) - **7th** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) (self only), [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662) (2 slots) **A Fairer Face** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The mirror seer chooses a creature within 100 feet that can see its own reflection in a mirror. The creature must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by their reflection for 1 minute. The creature can attempt a new save to end the effect at the end of each of its turns. **Hall of Mirrors** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The mirror seer causes all surfaces in a 30-foot burst within 100 feet to become reflective for 1 minute. Every creature in the area or that later enters the area must succeed at a DC 27 Will save or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) by the reflections and refractions. The confusion ends if the creature leaves the area, and the creature can attempt a new save to end the effect at the end of each of its turns. When the effect ends for a creature, that creature becomes temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
The Mirror Gazes Back The mirror seer's great power stems from their [_malefic mirror_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3590). The story of the being and power behind the mirror can be told in many ways. Is it a [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) trapped in the confines of the item, loathing its prison and yearning to be released? An entity working its will in our world through the mirror seer? Both of these have been true of mirror seers in history. And those who defeat a mirror seer might find themselves speaking to the mirror. Or even making their own pact, secure that they, surely, can avoid being corrupted...
name: Mirror Seer

markdown:
The summoners called god callers have a magical link to eidolons, revered as gods by the people of Sarkoris. Though this NPC is based on Sarkorian god callers, they can be adapted to different types of summoners by changing out the eidolon for another creature and making thematic tweaks to skills and spells. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [God Caller](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3542) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 102 **Perception** +19 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet (with eidolon only) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +15 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Bonded Eidolon** The god caller fights alongside a mystical ally called an eidolon, most likely the [beast eidolon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3679). The eidolon has the standard number of actions, uses its normal stat block, and counts toward the encounter's XP budget normally. The eidolon must remain within 100 feet of the god caller, or its physical form will dissolve. The god caller can make their eidolon take form or disappear with the Manifest Eidolon action. **Items** _+1 [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=39)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [war flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=402)_, [_wand of environmental endurance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1517) --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +16 **Will** +19 **HP** 150 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _war flail_ +21 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 2d10+10 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning **Primal Spontaneous Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [Gouging Claw](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) - **4th** [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748), [Weapon Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758) (2 slots) - **5th** [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559), [Impaling Spike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1571) (2 slots) **Beseech the Spirits** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The god caller reaches out to local entities for enhanced perception and perspective. The god caller gains [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet and [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) for 10 minutes. The god caller can't use this ability again until after propitiating the spirits during their next daily preparation. **Manifest Eidolon** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The god caller causes their eidolon to manifest in a space adjacent to them if it's unmanifested, or to unmanifest and disappear from physical reality if it was already manifested. **Tandem Trick** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The god caller uses a team tactic with their eidolon, chosen from the following list, with the listed number of actions and traits.
  • **Enlarge** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The god caller casts [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514) on their eidolon even if the eidolon is beyond range or line of effect. The god caller doesn't need to expend a spell slot, and can choose 2nd or 4th rank.
  • **Tandem Strike** The god caller makes a Strike and their eidolon can Strike as a reaction. Both attacks count toward the god caller's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until both attacks have been made.
  • **Transfer** The god caller transfers 50 HP from themself to their eidolon or vice versa. If the creature losing HP has 50 HP or fewer, this effect transfers as many HP as possible without reducing that creature below 1 HP.
  • **Transpose** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The god caller and their eidolon teleport to swap places.
God Callers And The Divine Some spirits called by god callers of Sarkoris are divine beings capable of granting spells. Consider granting a god caller NPC a cleric focus spell appropriate to one of the god's domains if they worship such a deity (using the same DC and spell attack as their primal spells). For instance, the [Stag Mother of the Forest of Stones](/Deities.aspx?ID=609) might grant the [_savor the sting_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1826) domain spell from the [pain](/Domains.aspx?ID=87) domain.
name: God Caller

Ghosts and other spirits swirl around the spirit binder, creating a constant aura of flickering faces and forms. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Spirit Binder](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3543) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 103 **Perception** +20; spiritsense (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21) **Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +22, [Spirits Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Spiritsense** ([detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The spirit binder can sense the spirits of creatures, including living creatures, most non-mindless [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), and [haunts](/Traits.aspx?ID=622) within the listed range. Since spiritsense detects spiritual essence, not physical bodies, it can detect spirits projected by spells (such as [_project image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1640)) or possessing otherwise soulless objects. It can't detect soulless bodies, [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=563), or objects, and like most senses, it doesn't penetrate through solid objects. --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +19 **Will** +24 **HP** 175 **Haunting Spirits** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) 30 feet. The spirits bound by a spirit binder swirl around, lashing out at their foes. An enemy that enters or starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 27 Will save or take 3d6 spirit damage and be [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (double damage and frightened 2 on a critical failure).
 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** ghost claw +19 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 2d10+6 slashing plus 2d6 spirit **Ranged** spirit pitch +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)), **Damage** 3d6 spirit plus 2d6 [persistent spirit](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 31, attack +23 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **1st** [Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (3 slots) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [Ghostly Carrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=1543), [Peaceful Rest](/Spells.aspx?ID=1625) (3 slots) - **3rd** [Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [Ghostly Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1544), [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1584) (3 slots) - **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) (3 slots) - **5th** [Invoke Spirits](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578), [Spiritual Guardian](/Spells.aspx?ID=1688), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) (3 slots) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685) (2 slots) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to or from the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) only) **Spirit Scrying** The spirit binder's scrying spells can target or detect [spirits](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) on other planes as though the spirits were in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). **Succumb to the Void** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The spirit binder taps into the more nefarious spirits of the Void, becoming something morbid and cruel. For 1d4 rounds, their resistance, aura of spirits, Strikes, and spirit spells change their damage type from spirit damage to void damage and replace their [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) trait with the [void](/Domains.aspx?ID=56) trait.
name: Spirit Binder

<title level="1" pfs="">[Curse Monger](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3544)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Oracles who have been consumed by their visions and the divine gifts bestowed upon them turn to more sinister paths, becoming curse mongers. Seeking to rid themselves of the shadows that haunt them, curse mongers lash out and attempt to bind others to their fate.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 37

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Curse_Monger.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 14">[Curse Monger](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3544)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 104

**Perception** +23

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +29, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +5

**Con** +3

**Int** +8

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Incurable Curse** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566)) The curse monger is permanently [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) by a curse that can't be removed from them in any way. The GM chooses the condition and decides whether the curse is [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), or [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676).

**Items**
_+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=39)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) [sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364)_, [_scroll of fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 35 

**Fort** +23 

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +26 

–2 to all saves vs. [curses](/Traits.aspx?ID=566)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 230

</row>

**Cursed Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 30 feet. The very earth and air around the curse monger are poisoned by the curses that burden their soul. Any creature who enters or starts their turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 31 Will save or be [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) (or doomed 2 on a critical failure). Regardless of the result of its save, the creature is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_sickle_ +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 2d4+13 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+13 bludgeoning

**Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 37, attack +29
- **Cantrips (7th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
- **1st**
[Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566) (3 slots)
- **2nd**
[Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [Laughing Fit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=1623) (3 slots)
- **3rd**
[Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1563), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (3 slots)
- **4th**
[Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=1454), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) (3 slots)
- **5th**
[False Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=1522), [Mariner's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1593), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) (3 slots)
- **6th**
[Never Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630), [Spellwrack](/Spells.aspx?ID=1683) (3 slots)
- **7th**
[Cursed Metamorphosis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479), [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Possession](/Spells.aspx?ID=1638) (3 slots)

**Share Burden** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566)) The curse monger shares their awful burden with one creature they can see within 120 feet. The target must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or be afflicted with the same condition as the curse monger's incurable curse for 24 hours. On a critical failure, the curse's value is 2. The curse lasts for 24 hours but can be removed (unlike the incurable curse), and ends if the curse monger dies. This action has the same tradition trait as incurable curse.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Jinxed Curse Mongers</title>

For certain curse mongers, spreading the curse is an involuntary part of the curse itself. When a jinxed curse monger starts their turn, Share Burden automatically attempts to curse a random creature in range that's not already cursed; this doesn't require an action. If the attempt fails, the curse monger must spend their first actions on that turn casting a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) spell ([_cursed metamorphosis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479), [_never mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614), [_spellwrack_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1683), [_mariner's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1593), [_outcast's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), or [_ill omen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566)). If the curse monger doesn't want to curse anyone, the GM determines a target at random. The target doesn't have to be an enemy but can't be the curse monger.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-544" />
name: Curse Monger

<title level="1" pfs="">[Enchanting Ritualist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3545)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Possessing great stores of knowledge on mental magic and rituals, enchanting ritualists can also be solitary and capricious, tricking and transforming their visitors. The GM can change the selection of rituals this NPC knows freely, choosing any rituals of 9th rank or lower.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 40

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Enchanting_Ritualist.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 18">[Enchanting Ritualist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3545)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 105

**Perception** +31

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +34, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +31, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +35, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +34, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +36, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +34

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +6

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +8
</row>

**Ritual Caster** the enchanting ritualist gains a +2 circumstance to skill checks for rituals

**Items**
_+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=357)_, _[greater bands of force](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3058)_, ornate ritual book

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 40 

**Fort** +27 

**Ref** +28 

**Will** +33 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 320

</row>

**Resistances**
[mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 20

**Aura of Contentment** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 30 feet. A creature that enters or starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 38 Will save or lose the desire to do anything except rest and relax. [Hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) taken against creatures affected by the aura end the effect. If a creature in the aura succeeds on their Will save or is the subject of a hostile action, it's temporarily immune to the aura of contentment for 24 hours. The enchanting ritualist can exempt creatures from the aura's effects.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_club_ +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 3d6+12 bludgeoning plus 2d6 mental and fool's feast

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+12 bludgeoning plus 2d6 mental and fool's feast

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
enchanting wisps +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 9d6 mental plus fool's feast

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_club_ +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 3d6+12 bludgeoning plus 2d6 mental and fool's feast

**Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 42, attack +34
- **Cantrips (9th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)
- **1st**
[Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1439), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569) (4 slots)
- **2nd**
[Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [Darkvision](/Spells.aspx?ID=1481), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [Status](/Spells.aspx?ID=1690) (4 slots)
- **3rd**
[Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503), [Hypnotize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564), [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1584) (4 slots)
- **4th**
[Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Honeyed Words](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (4 slots)
- **5th**
[Dreaming Potential](/Spells.aspx?ID=1504), [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1551), [Scouting Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665) (4 slots)
- **6th**
[Mislead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1605), [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1650), [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [Zealous Conviction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1760) (4 slots)
- **7th**
[Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=1595), [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=1640), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) (4 slots)
- **8th**
[Canticle of Everlasting Grief](/Spells.aspx?ID=1459), [Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644), [Spirit Song](/Spells.aspx?ID=2029), [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1730) (4 slots)
- **9th**
[Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537), [Overwhelming Presence](/Spells.aspx?ID=1621), [Synesthesia](/Spells.aspx?ID=2035), [Telepathic Demand](/Spells.aspx?ID=2036) (4 slots)

**Rituals** DC 44

- **2nd**
[Heartbond](/Rituals.aspx?ID=33)
- **3rd**
[Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12)
- **4th**
[Atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2), [Rest Eternal](/Rituals.aspx?ID=36)
- **5th**
[Astral Projection](/Rituals.aspx?ID=29), [Fortifying Brew](/Rituals.aspx?ID=147), [Call Spirit](/Rituals.aspx?ID=5), [Planar Servitor](/Rituals.aspx?ID=120), [Resurrect](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20)
- **6th**
[Binding Circle](/Rituals.aspx?ID=110), [Commune](/Rituals.aspx?ID=6)
- **7th**
[Collective Memories](/Rituals.aspx?ID=113), [Planar Displacement](/Rituals.aspx?ID=119)
- **9th**
[Fantastic Facade](/Rituals.aspx?ID=32)

**Fool's Feast**   Recipients of the ritualist's generosity pay dearly if the ritualist decides to turn against them. The ritualist gets a +4 circumstance bonus to attack rolls against any creature that has willingly participated in or benefited from one of their spells or rituals conducted in the last 12 hours. A creature that didn't help conduct a ritual still qualifies if it benefited in other ways, such as drinking a serving of [_fortifying brew_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=147).

 When the enchanting ritualist damages such a creature with a Strike, the target is affected by a 9th-rank [_cursed metamorphosis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479) spell (DC 42). If the Strike was a critical hit, the creature gets a degree of success one worse than it rolled. Once a creature succeeds at a save against this spell, it is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

</column>

markdown:
Barristers may serve as criminal prosecutors or legal advocates, defending the rights of those accused of crimes or named as defendants in civil cases. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 13 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8 [Barrister](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3546) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 108 **Perception** +6 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +10, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +9 **Str** +0 **Dex** +1 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Legal Specialist** In a court case or other legal proceeding, the barrister is a 4thlevel challenge.
**Sway the Judge and Jury** A barrister gains a +2 circumstance bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) something of the deciding members within a courtroom. If the barrister successfully [Performs](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401) against a DC of 20 during the 20 minutes prior to the check, they increase the circumstance bonus to +4. **Items** court garb (functions as [fine clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715)), law book (functions as [scholarly journal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2763)), [Writing Set](/Equipment.aspx?ID=63) --- **AC** 13 **Fort** +3 **Ref** +3 **Will** +12 **HP** 8 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +4 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning **Cite Precedent** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) The barrister uses existing case law to undermine their opposition. If they succeed at a DC 20 [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check, they impose a –2 circumstance penalty on the next [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check an opponent attempts in a legal argument. Any further attempts to Cite Precedent fail until a new topic with different precedents is being argued.
name: Barrister

markdown:
Properly exercised, the duties of a judge include strict adherence to the law regardless of station, with minimal sentimentality. Yet for every unbiased justice, there's one who is zealously confident in their own agenda. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 13 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8 [Judge](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3547) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 108 **Perception** +8; (15 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +8, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +14 **Str** +0 **Dex** -1 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Group Impression** When the judge [Makes an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), they can compare their [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check result to the Will DCs of up to four targets instead of one.
**Legal Specialist** In a legal proceeding, the judge is a 6th-level challenge. **Items** gavel (functions as a [club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=357)), judge's robes, _Law and Rhetoric_ --- **AC** 13 **Fort** +5 **Ref** +1 **Will** +12 **HP** 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** gavel +4, **Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +4 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning **Ranged** gavel +3 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning **Remember, You're Under Oath** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The judge reminds a creature of the oath they swore to the court. The judge makes an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check against the target' s Will DC. On a success, the target takes a –2 status penalty to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) for 10 minutes (or a –4 status penalty on a critical success). Regardless of the result, the target is temporarily immune to this ability for 24 hours.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Tax Collector](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3548)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ledgers and marks, positive and negative flows, and levies and allotments are a tax collector's daily bread. Where coin is gained, from whom it's collected, and to whom it's disbursed are their concerns—not who can afford the taxes.

<column gap="tiny">

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

</column>

</column>

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +1

**Con** +0

**Int** +3

**Cha** +2

**Financial Specialist** When dealing with matters of taxes and finance, the tax collector is a 3rd-level challenge.

**Items**
[Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (10 bolts), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), collection of expired documents with intact seals, [Merchant's Scale](/Equipment.aspx?ID=34), [Padded Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=3), tax documents in scroll case

</column>

**Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
crossbow +5 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Glittering Distraction** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The tax collector Strides. At any point during this movement, they can Interact to hurl a handful of coins. If there are commoners about, this typically causes a scene. Crowds are usually [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and [have other effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2796).

markdown:
The mayor is the political leader of a settlement. While not always an elected position, it usually involves devoting time to both civic and ceremonial functions and knowing the needs of their settlement. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Mayor](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3549) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 109 **Perception** +9 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); up to 2 additional languages spoken in their settlement **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +15, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +15, [Guild Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +13 **Str** +0 **Dex** +2 **Con** +1 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Political Specialist** For encounters involving seeking political favors, the mayor is a 6th-level challenge.
**Pulse of the Electorate** The mayor can quickly find things out, and 1 hour after anyone in their settlement becomes aware of an event or activity, the mayor becomes aware of it, so long as they have had time to hobnob with their constituents. **Items** decorative sword of station (functions as [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398)) --- **AC** 14 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +3 **Will** +14 **HP** 16 **But Will It Lose Me Votes** **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** A creature succeeds (but doesn't critically succeed) at a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check to make a [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) of the mayor; **Effect** The triggering creature (or one of its allies) must attempt the check again within the next hour, this time against the mayor's [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) DC. Society or a relevant [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill may be used for this check instead of Diplomacy. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** decorative sword of station +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Melee** fist +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning

markdown:
The prime minister is the leader of a nation's bureaucracy. They are experienced politicians in charge of the laws and regulations of their territory, answering only to a monarch, if there is one. Often, though, a vote of “No Confidence” or similar legal procedure exists for removing the prime minister. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Prime Minister](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3550) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 110 **Perception** +9; (19 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); up to 3 additional languages spoken in their nation **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +22, [Guild Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +19, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +22 **Str** +0 **Dex** +2 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Political Specialist** For encounters involving politics, the prime minister is a 10th-level challenge.
**Unshakable Confidence** All attempts to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=3028) the prime minister have a result one degree worse. **Items** [Rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36) --- **AC** 14 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +3 **Will** +19 **HP** 15 **Cutting Counterpoint** **Trigger** The prime minister hears a creature attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), or an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check against any creature other than the prime minister; **Effect** The prime minister interrupts with a witty barb, cutting the credibility of the creature's argument. The prime minister attempts their own check of the same type. If the result is higher than that of the triggering check, the triggering check is considered a failure regardless of its roll.
In extended negotiations, like a [Victory Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) challenge, the prime minister can't use this ability again until every creature in the discussion has had an opportunity to attempt a check (even if they decide not to make one). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** rapier +5 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Melee** fist +5 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Raise The Alarm!</title>

In a settlement with an alarm, brawls, or other major disruptions trigger an alarm 1 round after the watch is alerted. Guards start to arrive after about 5 rounds, usually in patrols of 2 or 3 members, with larger groups of 8–12 near important locations.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Harbormaster](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3553)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A port must sustain itself on different types of flows: the seaward and coastal flows, marked by the rising and falling of the tides, and the constant flow of trade. A harbormaster is expected to know the former reflexively and encourage the latter within the jurisdictional rules of law.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 111

**Perception** +6

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); up to 2 additional languages

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +5, [Fishing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +5, [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** +2

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Steady Balance** Whenever the harbormaster rolls a success on a check to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369), they get a critical success instead. They're not [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) while Balancing on [narrow surfaces](/Rules.aspx?ID=455) and [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=456).

**Items**
[Fishing Tackle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=20), [Hatchet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=27) (2), ledger, [Manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32), [Spyglass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=54)

</column>

---

fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hatchet +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d6+7 slashing

**Experienced Hand**   The harbormaster has endured their share of adverse conditions at sea. Any creature that's in adverse weather or aboard a vessel on rough water is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the harbormaster.

</column>

Inspectors cultivate a wide selection of skills to investigate arson, murder, and other serious crimes, usually in major urban centers. They can assist adventurers, perhaps noticing an object or creature that seems out of the ordinary without being sure why. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Inspector](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3554) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 112 **Perception** +12; (15 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302), [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301), or [Search](/Actions.aspx?ID=2630)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); up to 3 additional languages **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +8, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +12 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Investigation Specialist** For encounters involving investigation, the inspector is a 5th-level challenge.
**Sense Demise** The inspector can [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) on a corpse, learning about the creature in the moments before its death. **Items** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43) --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +5 **Ref** +10 **Will** +12 **HP** 40 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** shortsword +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing plus 1 precision **Melee** fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning plus 1 precision **Unavoidable Question** ([Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) **Frequency** once per turn; **Effect** The inspector [Demoralizes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) a creature and asks a question. On a success, the next Strike the inspector attempts against that target deals an additional 1d6 precision damage. If the target spends an action on their next turn to answer the question, either truthfully or by succeeding at a DC 25 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check, they are temporarily immune to the inspector's Unavoidable Question for 1 minute.
name: Inspector

<title level="1" pfs="">[Jailer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3555)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A jailer's primary responsibility is to keep prisoners from escaping. Jailers must often use force, or the threat of force, to keep their charges in line, as even the most carefully crafted cells, manacles, or chains can fail with time and persistence when the prisoners have the will to attempt an escape.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

</column>

</column>

<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 112

**Perception** +9; (10 to find concealed objects) 

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +5, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +7

**Cha** +0

</column>

---

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 45

</row>

**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
club +11,
**Damage** 1d6+8 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
club +10 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d6+8 bludgeoning

**Efficient Capture** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The jailer has [manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2733) in hand and is adjacent to a creature; **Effect** The jailer attempts to bind the creature's wrists or ankles with the manacles. If the jailer succeeds at an attack roll with a +9 modifier against the target's AC, they apply the manacles.

**Intimidating Strike** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The jailer makes a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, the target is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), or frightened 2 on a critical hit.

**Subdue Prisoners**   The jailer doesn't take the normal penalty for making a [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attack when attacking with their club.

</column>

Watch officers get the job done, though their methods aren't always gentle or kind. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Watch Officer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3556) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 113 **Perception** +8; (9 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +6, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +9, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +7, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +5 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 **Items** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), [Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (20 bolts), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [Signal Whistle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=50), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10), [warhammer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=24) --- **AC** 19 (21 with shield raised) **Fort** +10 **Ref** +6 **Will** +8 **HP** 45 **Air of Authority** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 10 feet. Creatures in the aura who are the same or lower level than the watch officer take a –2 status penalty to their Will DC against the watch officer's attempts to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) or [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) them.
**Bravery** When the watch officer rolls a success on a Will save against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effect, they get a critical success instead. In addition, any time they gain the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition, reduce its value by 1.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** warhammer +13 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 1d8+7 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning **Ranged** crossbow +10 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d8+3 piercing **Sudden Charge** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The watch officer Strides twice. If they end their movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, they can make a melee Strike against that enemy.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Equestrian Constable](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3557)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Equestrian constables patrol for criminals on horseback in wealthy areas or serve as reeves to enforce court orders. Some patrol major roads far from the protection of the city guard.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 113

**Perception** +11

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +10, [Settlement Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Trained Animal** The equestrian constable rides a trained mount of their level or lower, usually a [war horse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3060) or, for [elite equestrian constables](/Rules.aspx?ID=790), a [veteran war horse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3677). The animal has the standard number of actions, uses its normal stat block, and counts toward the encounter's XP budget normally.

**Items**
[Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (20 bolts), [Guisarme](/Weapons.aspx?ID=25), [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12), [poor manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32), [Rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44), [Signal Whistle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=50)

On a success, the triggering action is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2342).

markdown:
Garrisons of professional guards are given the duties of patrolling the streets, assisting citizenry, and acting as a quick military response in times of crisis. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [City Guard Squadron](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3558) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 114 **Perception** +12 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +11, [Settlement Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +9 **Str** +5 **Dex** +0 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **Seek Quarry** City guards can spend 1 minute to designate a single creature for whom they have a physical description as their quarry. They gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks against their quarry. --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +9 **Will** +11 **HP** 75 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; city passage, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **City Passage** City guards ignore [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) caused by crowds or from movement through narrow spaces such as alleyways. **Lower Halberds!** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The city guards engage in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a 10-foot emanation, with a DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d10 piercing or slashing damage
1d10+7 piercing or slashing damage
1d10+10 piercing or slashing damage **Shoot Crossbows!** The city guards draw or reload their crossbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a 10-foot burst within 120 feet that deals 2d8 piercing damage with a DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. When the city guards are reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst.
Local Guards The city guard squadron depicted here consists of humans armed with halberds and crossbows. You can alter these details to match the settlements in your game, adding ancestry traits, regional languages, and other abilities as necessary, and swapping out their weaponry for ones appropriate to the local culture. Some of the ancestry entries in chapter 2 also include troops even more suited to settlements inhabited by different ancestries!
name: City Guard Squadron

A watchmage uses a mix of magic and martial training to enforce the law. They magically detect invisible criminals, locate stolen property, and counter illegal spells. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Watchmage](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3559) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 115 **Perception** +15 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +9, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +11 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 **Arcane Watch** The watchmage can either [Investigate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2627) or [Search](/Actions.aspx?ID=2630) while using the [Detect Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2624) exploration activity. **Items** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=77) (20 arrows), spellbook (contains all prepared spells plus [_earthbind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506), [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675), and [_tailwind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1711)) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +12 **Will** +14 **HP** 70 **Counter Escape** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** A creature Casts a Spell with the [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) trait or as a reaction; **Effect** The watchmage expends a spell slot of the same rank or higher as the trigger creature's spell and attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell (counteract modifier +11). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d6+5 bludgeoning **Ranged** shortbow +15 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing plus 1d6 force **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 20, attack +12 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) - **2nd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677) **Eldritch Arms** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) In a brief ritual that takes 10 minutes, the watchmage chooses a single weapon or unarmed attack through which they can focus their magic. Strikes the watchmage makes with that weapon are magical and deal 1d6 additional force damage. **Spellbound Strike** **Requirements** The watchmage is wielding the weapon chosen with Eldritch Arms; **Effect** The watchmage Casts a Spell that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast, imbuing that spell into the weapon. The watchmage Strikes with the required weapon. This counts as two attacks for the watchmage's multiple attack penalty. On a hit, the target is also affected by the spell, though the target gets any normal defenses allowed by the spell.
If the spell is targeted, it targets the creature that was hit and no one else. If the spell is an area, the target must be in the area. A burst is centered on a corner of the target's square if the target is Medium or smaller, or the corner of a square closest to the creature's center if it's Large or larger. A cone or line emits from a square of the watchmage's choice adjacent to the target.
name: Watchmage

The captain of the guard leads a troop of soldiers who serve as security forces for a powerful individual, most often a high-ranking noble or very rich merchant, though this stat block could also represent a lower-ranking captain of the guard for the leader of a nation. A formidable opponent in their own right, the captain of the guard skillfully employs their troops to protect the life and health of their ward. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Captain of the Guard](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3560) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 115 **Perception** +15 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +10, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +8 **Str** +5 **Dex** +0 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (20 bolts), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13), _+1 [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_, [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10) --- **AC** 24 (26 with shield raised) **Fort** +14 **Ref** +12 **Will** +15 **HP** 95 **Aura of Command** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 30 feet. The captain of the guard bolsters lower-level guards under their command, granting them a +1 status bonus to their attack rolls and a +2 status bonus to their Will saves.
**Bravery** When the captain rolls a success on a Will save against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effect, they get a critical success instead. In addition, any time they gain the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition, reduce its value by 1.
**Shield Warden** When the captain has their shield raised, they can [Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75) when an attack is made against an adjacent ally. If they do, the shield prevents that ally from taking damage instead of the captain.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _longsword_ +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d8+11 slashing **Melee** fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+11 bludgeoning **Ranged** crossbow +12 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d8+6 piercing **Shielded Advance** **Requirements** The captain of the guard has their shield raised; **Effect** The captain of the guard presses forward, using their shield to push back foes. The captain Strides and [Shoves](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), in either order. The multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Shove, though the Shove does count toward the captain's multiple attack penalty.
name: Captain of the Guard

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Executioners carry out sentences from cruel tyrants and legitimate rulers alike. Most remain numb to the necessity of their duty, but some evil executioners grow to love the power of having someone else's life in their hands.

<column gap="tiny">

_greataxe_ +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 1d12+9 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+9 bludgeoning

**Behead** <actions string="Three Actions" />  **Requirements** The executioner is adjacent to a [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) creature or a creature specifically prepared for a killing blow; **Effect** The executioner Strikes the creature with their greataxe. On a hit, in addition to taking damage, the target must attempt a DC 23 Fortitude save or be reduced to 0 HP and become dying 1. If the creature was already dying (including if it was reduced to 0 HP by the Strike's damage), the creature's dying value increases by 1, in addition to any increase from the Strike. On a critical failure, the creature dies instantly. If the executioner's Strike was a critical hit, the target uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of their saving throw.

**Intimidating Strike** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The executioner makes a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, the target is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), or frightened 2 on a critical hit.

**Mark for Death** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The executioner marks a single creature they can see for death. The first time each round the executioner Strikes that creature, the Strike deals an extra 1d12 precision damage. The creature remains marked for death until the executioner is knocked out, marks a different creature for death, or the encounter ends.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Warden](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3562)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wardens are the chief guardians of borders and frontiers. Whether standing watch over a single village or an entire region, wardens keep a vigilant eye out for threats to their charges. Wardens are stalwart folk, often called to duty by Erastil to protect those around them. Be it times of war or along the frontier, wardens are often the balanced edge of a blade between a community and the wilds at its fringes.
