markdown:
Powerful governments retain gendarmes to guard important magistrates, enforce laws protecting national security, reinstate order amid unrest, and capture unusually dangerous criminals. They're also sent to deal with important cases in rural areas without substantial guards of their own. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Gendarme](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3563) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 117 **Perception** +19 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Items** _+1 [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=434)_ (20 arrows), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=374)_, _+1 [gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=359)_, [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12) --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +14 **Will** +17 (nerves of steel) **HP** 120 **Nerves of Steel** When the gendarme succeeds against a fear effect, they get a critical success instead.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** The gendarme can [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) instead of Striking. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _flail_ +19 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Melee** _gauntlet_ +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Free-Hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=613), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Ranged** _composite longbow_ +16 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 1d8+8 piercing **Stop in the Name of the Law!** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) The gendarme Strides twice and then [Demoralizes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395). On a success, the target is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) with a value equal to its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) value until it is no longer frightened. **Shoot Down** The gendarme carefully makes a ranged Strike. If the Strike deals damage, the target must succeed at a DC 26 Reflex saving throw or fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
name: Gendarme

These guards have been extensively trained to perform complex maneuvers together. They are sent to capture accused criminals believed to be especially dangerous (whether due to their own abilities or due to their allies). **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Arrester Squadron](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3564) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 118 **Perception** +17 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Settlement Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 **HP** 135 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Coordinated Step** The arrester squadron Steps twice. **Fire Longbows!** The arrester squadron fire a coordinated volley with their longbows against each enemy in a 10-foot burst within 150 feet that deals 3d8 piercing damage with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. When the arresters are reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Seize Them!** ([Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)) The arresters attack with saps and tackle foes. Each enemy in a 5-foot emanation must attempt a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage and additional effects depend on the number of actions. The DC to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) any of the following conditions is 26.
1d6+3 bludgeoning damage (plus [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) for 1 round on a critical failure)
3d6+6 bludgeoning damage (plus grabbed for 1 round on a failure or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) for 1 round on a critical failure)
4d6+9 bludgeoning damage (plus grabbed for 1 round on a failure or restrained for 1 round on a critical failure) **Sweep the Area** The arresters [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) in a 40-foot burst or 80-foot cone and [Point Out](/Actions.aspx?ID=2315) up to four targets.
name: Arrester Squadron

<title level="1" pfs="">[Accuser Agent](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3565)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Accuser agents might be high court advocates, official spymasters, or innocuous adjutants delivering important messages to magistrates, generals, officers, or mercenaries. They have ample latitude in matters of government security, though they sometimes have little oversight. When their findings demand an official response, accuser agents present cases before national tribunals or in royal courts.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

</column>

markdown:
The infernal registrar is a mortal representative of Hell's bureaucracy. They can access copies of all infernal contracts signed by a denizen of their world and can navigate the machinations of Hell's many devils. They can grant special access—for a price. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Infernal Registrar](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3566) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 119 **Perception** +19; (33 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +33, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +30, [Scribe Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +30, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +33 **Str** +2 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Contract Specialist** For encounters involving matters of contracts and dealings with [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), the infernal registrar is an 18th-level challenge.
**Death is a Promotion** The infernal registrar does not fear death, as they have a signed infernal contract for immediate promotion to a mid-ranked [devil](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=389) upon their soul's arrival in [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42). They're immune to all [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) involving threats of death.
**Friends in Low Places** Though devils do not respect most mortals, they respect the office of infernal registrar. No creature with the [devil](/Traits.aspx?ID=575) trait of 18th level or lower will knowingly and willingly attack an infernal registrar.
**Technically Correct** The infernal registrar uses their [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) modifier on all [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389). **Items** _staff of office_ (functions as _+1 [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)_) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +16 **Will** +33 +2 circumstance to all saves vs. [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **HP** 180 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _staff of office_ +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 2d4+8 bludgeoning plus 1d6 fire **Melee** fist +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **2nd** [Translate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) (at will) - **3rd** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1464), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Ring of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656) - **4th** [Detect Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1487), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) - **5th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [Divine Immolation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1496), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588) **A Favor for a Favor** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The infernal registrar has the authority to make limited infernal contracts with other mortals. They summon a contract with the legal language they desire. Detecting hidden clauses in the contract requires a successful DC 43 [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) or a DC 38 [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check. Once signed, the contract vanishes into the infernal filing system in Hell. The infernal registrar cannot grant any boons beyond their own personal power (usually limited to information, advice, or access to elements of the infernal bureaucracy). **Request Document** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The infernal registrar makes a request to summon a copy of any infernal contract a specific creature has signed. They must know enough information to specifically identify the individual who signed. The infernal registrar attempts a [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check with a DC equal to a [hard DC of the level of the creature in question](/Rules.aspx?ID=2627). The infernal registrar will never promise a successful use of this ability in the agreements they make. Each agreement is typically for one attempt. Any copy summoned is simply a copy, has no impact on the original contract if destroyed or altered, and will vanish if taken more than 20 feet from the infernal registrar.
**Critical Success** A copy of the contract appears before the infernal registrar after 10 minutes.
**Success** A copy of the contract appears before the infernal registrar after 1 hour.
**Failure** The attempt fails, but the infernal registrar can try again after 24 hours.
**Critical Failure** The attempt fails, and the infernal registrar can't try again for the named creature for 1 year.
The Devil Out Of The Details Instead of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), an infernal registrar might run the bureaucracy of a vast magical city or a commune of otherworldly entities. To represent this, replace any reference to devils with a more appropriate creature type, and replace the registrar's divine spells with arcane or occult spells, respectively. They also speak a more appropriate language, like [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) or [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164). Their contract abilities remain essentially the same, though details can be described differently.
name: Infernal Registrar

Governments often organize and deploy squadrons of watchmages in places where dangerous magic is expected. Members combine their spellcasting to cast at a higher level than they could alone. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Watchmage Squadron](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3567) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 120 **Perception** +22; invisibility scan **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +19 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Invisibility Scan** Invisibility can't make anything [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) or [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=98) to the watchmage squadron. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +16 **Will** +19 **HP** 180 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 26, attack +18 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) - **2nd** [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) - **4th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548) - **5th** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588), [Slither](/Spells.aspx?ID=1676) **Troop Spellcasting** When the watchmage squadron Casts a Spell, the individual members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a burst, cone, or line and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line. **Bash Heads** ([Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)) The watchmages lash out against all enemies in a 5-foot emanation with their fists, dealing 4d4+4 bludgeoning damage with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. **Fire Shortbows!** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The watchmages fire a volley against each enemy in a 10-foot burst within 150 feet, with a DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions. When the squadron is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst.
1d6+3 piercing plus 1d6 force damage
3d6+6 piercing plus 1d6 force damage
4d6+6 piercing plus 1d6 force damage
name: Watchmage Squadron

A grand inquisitor leads powerful governmental forces. They're often champions of oppressive empires or overzealous intelligence networks. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Grand Inquisitor](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3568) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 121 **Perception** +28; (31 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +27, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +30, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +28 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_, _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=393)_, _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=399)_ --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +20 (+23 vs. damaging effects) **Will** +28 **HP** 215 **Searching Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When an opponent ends its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 36 Will save or it becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (frightened 2 on a critical failure), and the grand inquisitor learns its surface thoughts (and underlying motive on a critical failure).
**Symbol of Loyalty** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 60 feet. Allies in the aura who are 14th level and lower and are loyal to the grand inquisitor's cause get a +3 status bonus to Will saves.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** If the grand inquisitor's attack hits and this reaction was triggered by a [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) creature, the triggering action is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2342). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _scimitar_ +30 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d6+15 slashing **Melee** _starknife_ +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+15 piercing **Melee** fist +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+15 bludgeoning **Ranged** _starknife_ +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+15 piercing **Condemn** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) The grand inquisitor [Demoralizes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395). On a success, the target is stunned with a value equal to its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition. **I Am the Law!** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) The grand inquisitor vows to bring down all the fury of a nation down upon their foes. Up to three lower-level allies within 60 feet of the grand inquisitor can use their reaction to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), Strike, or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) with a +2 status bonus. **Twisting Fear** The grand inquisitor's Strikes deal an extra 3d6 precision damage to [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) creatures.
name: Grand Inquisitor

<title level="1" pfs="">[Dancer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3569)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Dance can be used to tell stories, share emotions, provide entertainment, and display a performer's athletic ability.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Dancer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3569)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 124

**Perception** +4

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +7, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +6, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +6, [Theater Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +0

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Dance Specialist** For encounters involving contests of dancing, the dancer is a 5thlevel challenge.

**Items**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) (3), jewelry and clothes (worth 10 gp)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +3 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
foot +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 piercing

**Fascinating Dance** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The dancer Strides up to their Speed. Once during this movement, when the dancer is adjacent to a creature, the dancer can attempt to mesmerize that creature, who attempts a DC 17 Will save. On a failure, that creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with the dancer until the end of its next turn.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-546" />
name: Dancer

Acrobats perform feats of agility, balance, and strength. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Acrobat](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3570) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 124 **Perception** +6 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Circus Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +5, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +5, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +1 **Acrobatic Specialist** For encounters involving contests of acrobatics and similar activities, the acrobat is a 5th-level challenge.
**Steady Balance** When the acrobat rolls a success on an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check, they get a critical success instead. They aren't off-guard when attempting to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) and can attempt an Acrobatics check instead of a Reflex save to [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313). **Items** [Climbing Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=14), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) (5), [Rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44) (50 feet) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +11 **Will** +4 **HP** 30 **Cat Fall** The acrobat treats all falls as 25 feet shorter.
**Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** The acrobat is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker they can see; **Effect** The acrobat gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** dagger +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing **Melee** foot +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)), **Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning **Ranged** dagger +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing **Sneak Attack** The acrobat deals an extra 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. **Swinging Strike** The acrobat swings on a rope or trapeze, moving up to double their Speed. At any point during the swing, they can make a melee Strike.
name: Acrobat

<title level="1" pfs="">[Street Musician](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3571)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Many musicians make their living off stage by playing at markets, fairs, or crossroads. While their fame may not be as widespread as theatrical performers, they are nonetheless staples of many communities.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Street_Musician.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Street Musician](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3571)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 125

**Perception** +9

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +6, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +5, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +8, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +8, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +8, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Items**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [Musical Instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37) (handheld)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +9 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 32

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 piercing

**Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 18, attack +10
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Summon Instrument](/Spells.aspx?ID=1703)
- **1st**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) (3 slots)

**Distracting Drone** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The street musician is playing their instrument; **Effect** The street musician attempts a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check compared to the Will DC of an observer within 30 feet. On a success, the target is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by the street musician and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.

**Sneak Attack**   The street musician deals an additional 1d4 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. This increases to 1d6 against creatures off-guard due to the street musician's [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390) or distracting drone.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-546" />
name: Street Musician

<title level="1" pfs="">[Juggler](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3572)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Jugglers are physical performers who master the art of manipulating props. Usually, this involves throwing multiple objects up in a flowing pattern, but some use ricocheting items, spinning items, or other objects to keep them aloft.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Juggler.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Juggler](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3572)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 125

**Perception** +9

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Circus Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +8, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Juggling Specialist** For encounters involving juggling and other circus acts, the juggler is a 5th-level challenge.

**Items**
juggling club (3, functions as a [light hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=384)), [Dart](/Weapons.aspx?ID=68) (10), [Torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60) (3)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 30

</row>

**Return Throw** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A physical ranged attack with a throwing weapon critically fails to hit the juggler; **Effect** The juggler snatches the weapon from the air and immediately makes a ranged Strike against the attacker using that weapon.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
juggling club +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dart +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
juggling club +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
torch +10 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning plus 1 fire

**Juggle** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The juggler begins juggling up to three items of light or negligible Bulk. They can choose items in their hands or Interact to draw items on their person or pick up unattended items in reach. While juggling, they can Interact to add up to two items to their juggle, though they must drop an item for each one they add. The juggler is wielding all items they juggle, but the only actions they can take that require their hands are Return Throw, Juggling Bounce, Strike using a juggled weapon, Interact to add items to their juggle, or Dismiss to stop juggling. When the juggler Dismisses Juggle, they can choose to continue to wield, drop, or stow each juggled item, though they can't wield more items than they have hands. If at any point the juggler isn't wielding any items or becomes [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), the juggle ends and the juggler drops all the items.

**Juggling Bounce** <actions string="Single Action" />  The juggler Strikes with a thrown weapon they're juggling. If the Strike hits, the weapon bounces to a different creature in the weapon's first range increment. The juggler repeats the Strike, which uses the same multiple attack penalty and doesn't increase their multiple attack penalty.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Juggling Props</title>

The weapons presented in the juggler stat block are examples, but other good options include [starknives](/Weapons.aspx?ID=399), [hatchets](/Weapons.aspx?ID=382), [daggers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358), and even [alchemical bombs](/Weapons.aspx?ID=431). Practically any item of light Bulk can work. Jugglers are proficient in any ranged Strikes they make with thrown weapons. Higher level versions of this NPC might increase their maximum number of props to four or five.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-546" />
name: Juggler

markdown:
Mimes are performers who use movement, gestures, and expressions without any speech to act out a scene or situation for onlookers. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Mime](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3573) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 126 **Perception** +11 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); sign language **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Mimicry Specialist** For encounters involving mimicry or pantomime, the mime is a 6th-level challenge. --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +9 **Will** +12 **HP** 45 **Resistances** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 5 **Skill Mimicry** The mime receives a +1 circumstance bonus to skill checks to perform actions they have witnessed another creature successfully perform in the last minute, or +2 if they witness a creature critically succeed instead.
**Versatile Performance** The mime can use [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) instead of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), instead of [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395), and instead of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+5 bludgeoning **Mimic Assault** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** A creature damaged the mime with a weapon Strike since their previous turn; **Effect** The mime makes a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check against the Perception DC of the creature who damaged them, gesturing as if making an attack with the same weapon. On a success, the mime deals two dice of damage to the creature, using the same type and die size as the required weapon Strike. **Pantomime** ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The mime uses exaggerated movements to emulate one of the following effects, which lasts until the end of their next turn. Any creature who sees this ability can attempt to disbelieve this ability as it is used with a DC 14 Will save. Creatures that disbelieve are temporarily immune to pantomime for 1 minute.
_Barrier_: The mime creates an invisible 10-foot-by-10-foot stretch of wall adjacent to them and within their reach. The wall has AC 10, 5 hardness, and 10 HP. If the mime Sustains this effect, they can add an additional wall in the same manner.
_Rope_: The mime tugs an invisible rope, trying to knock over or pull at a creature within 15 feet. If the creature fails to disbelieve the pantomime, the mime can choose to either knock the creature [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) or to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) it 5 feet towards them.
_Wind_: The mime creates a 30-foot line of imaginary wind. Creatures who don't disbelieve the pantomime treat this area as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453), and if they enter or begin their turn in the area, they fall prone.
name: Mime

The life of a traveling actor is, contrary to belief, not one of glamor but effort. To be on the road going from town to town wearing a thousand faces invites little reward, save the adoration of the crowd. Even so, this is where they thrive. Actors typically travel in troupes, composed not only of other actors but also of stagehands, drivers, and assorted hangers-on. All of them fall under the thumb of a singular director, acting as both parent and manager to all within the troupe. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Traveling Actor](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3574) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 127 **Perception** +12 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); up to 4 other languages **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +7, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +10, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +9, [Theater Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +9 **Str** +2 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Padded Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=3), wooden sword (functions as a [light mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=360)) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +12 **Will** +9 **HP** 35 **Dramatic Death** **Trigger** The traveling actor takes any damage; **Effect** The traveling actor falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) and dramatically announces their death. They appear to have died. Anyone who is suspicious of this “death” can [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) to attempt a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=690) Perception check against the traveling actor's [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) DC. On a success, they see through the ruse.
**Versatile Performance** The traveling actor can use [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) instead of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) and instead of [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** wooden sword +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning **Overacted Strike** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The traveling actor puts all their expertise into an attack that strikes fear in those who witness it. The traveling actor Strikes. On a success, the traveling actor chooses another creature within 30 feet who can see the attack, who becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (or frightened 2 on a critical success).
name: Traveling Actor

<title level="1" pfs="">[Beast Tamer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3576)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Beast tamers bring the wild to civilization, rearing and training creatures to follow their commands and perform flashy tricks that entertain audiences. The beast tamer typically fights alongside a trained animal ally of its level or lower, most likely a [tiger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2867).

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Beast Tamer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3576)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 128

**Perception** +12

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Wildsong](/Languages.aspx?ID=116)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Circus Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +6, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +12, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Wild Empathy** The beast tamer can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) and to make very simple [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) of them.

**Items**
animal claw necklace ([primal symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2743)), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44) (50 feet), [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 55

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
whip +12 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 slashing

**Primal Prepared Spells** DC 22, attack +13
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713)
- **1st**
[Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=1547), [Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=1580), [Runic Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657)
- **2nd**
[Animal Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1440), [Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=1441), [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680)

**Druid Order Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 22
- **1st**
[Heal Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1855)

**Animal Trick** <actions string="Single Action" />  The beast tamer gains the support benefit appropriate to its trainedanimal companion. Unlike for an animal companion, this doesn't require the animal to use any of its actions. The benefit for a tiger is as follows: Until the start of the beast tamer's next turn, their Strikes that deal damage to a creature within the tiger's reach make the target [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of the beast tamer's next turn.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Ethical Taming</title>

Throughout the history of Golarion many entertainers thought very little about the conditions of the animals they kept. However, this is very rarely the case today. In fact, it is not uncommon that show animals are treated better than some of the performers. They are trained with positive reinforcement learning, and once safety can be assured, they live outside of a cage with the trainer themselves.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-546" />
name: Beast Tamer

markdown:
On the surface, puppeteers are simply providers of entertainment to the masses. With their little puppets and simple stories, their pantomimes are fun for the whole family. However, some puppeteers have a secret. They can animate their puppets with magic, sending them out to cause all manner of mischief in the dead of night. Puppeteers of this ilk tend to travel by themselves, though they might be found as part of a traveling group if they become lonely. They may serve as an innocent front for more illicit dealings or as lackeys to a larger group of thieves and ne'er do wells. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Puppeteer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3577) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 129 **Perception** +14 **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +13, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +13, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +9 **Str** +1 **Dex** +2 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +4 **Puppets** The puppeteer has three animate puppets under their control—a smart puppet, a strong puppet, and a swift puppet. A puppet is a Tiny object that can be share a space with another creature. The usually begin combat in the puppeteer's space. A puppet has AC 23, Hardness 5, 20 Hit Points, and object immunities. If a puppet is destroyed, the puppeteer takes 15 [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) mental damage. A puppeteer can rebuild a puppet with 7 days of work. If the puppeteer dies while any of their puppets are still active, the active puppets become independent, but lose the will to fight in their grief. **Items** _+1 [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_, puppets --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +14 **Will** +15 **HP** 95 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _dagger_ +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+7 piercing **Melee** fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning **Ranged** _dagger_ +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+7 piercing **Manipulate Puppets** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The puppeteer pulls at invisible strings to control their puppets. Each puppet Strides up to 30 feet. Each puppet can then make a Strike as described below. Each attack counts towards the puppeteer's multiple attack penalty, but their penalty does not increase until all attacks have been made.
  • **Smart Puppet** The smart puppet is covered in runes that give it occult power. It makes a ranged Strike against a creature within 30 feet at a +15 attack modifier. A successful Strike deals 2d6 mental damage.
  • **Strong Puppet** The strong puppet wields a tiny sword and shield. It makes a melee Strike against a creature whose space it shares at a +15 attack modifier. A successful Strike deals 2d8 slashing damage. In addition, the strong puppet gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC for 1 round.
  • **Swift Puppet** The swift puppet wields two tiny daggers. It makes a melee Strike against a creature whose space it shares at a +15 attack modifier. A successful Strike deals 2d4 piercing damage. If the swift puppet hits a creature that was hit by another puppet this round, its Strike deals an additional 1d4 precision damage.
Alternative Puppets This alternate set of villainous puppets Strikes with a +15 attack modifier. **Fiend Puppet** The puppet makes a ranged Strike against a creature within 30 feet for 1d10 spirit damage, plus 1d4 spirit damage if the target is [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517).
**Poisoner Puppet** The puppet makes a melee Strike with a tiny syringe of poison against a creature whose space it shares, dealing 1d4 piercing damage plus 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Undead Puppet** The puppet makes a melee Strike against a creature whose space it shares, dealing 2d8 void damage and making the target [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (or frightened 2 on a critical hit).
name: Puppeteer

markdown:
Though court jesters are often put-upon as the targets of easy mockery and idle amusements, do not mistake their self-deprecation for weakness. Beneath, the jester hides malice, a sharp tongue, and even sharper knives. They can often be found entertaining the nobles of court or preparing their next japes. During the indiscreet hours of the night, they may be found in the company of servants and spymasters. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Court Jester](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3578) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 129 **Perception** +21 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); up to 4 others **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +19, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_ (3), face paints --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +19 **Will** +22 **HP** 170 **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 10 **Pointed Joke** The court jester can use [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) instead of [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395). --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _dagger_ +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+8 piercing plus 4d4 [persistent poison](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** fist +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** _dagger_ +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+8 piercing plus 4d4 [persistent poison](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Poisoned Blade** The jester coats their dagger in poison. These daggers inflict an additional 4d4 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The poison expires 1 hour after leaving the jester's possession. **No Peeking!** The jester blows chalk or face powder in an adjacent enemy's face. The target must make a DC 29 Fortitude saving throw.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** target is dazzled and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.
name: Court Jester

A maestro is a performer who has achieved true excellence. These virtuosos can inspire those around them to greater heights or strike fear in their enemies' hearts. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Maestro](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3579) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 130 **Perception** +22 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +23, [Music Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +19, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +30, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +21 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Bardic Lore** The maestro can [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) on any subject with a +19 modifier.
**Performing Specialist** For encounters involving acting, music, or storytelling, the maestro is a 15th-level challenge. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435)_ (30 arrows), _+1 [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_, lyre (_[moderate maestro's instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3026)_), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=391)_ --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +24 **Will** +21 +1 circumstance bonus to saves vs. [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697), or [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) **HP** 180 **Resolve** When the maestro rolls a success on a Will save, they get a critical success instead. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _rapier_ +24 ([deadly 1d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+10 piercing plus resonating weaponry **Melee** fist +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning **Ranged** _composite shortbow_ +24 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d6+9 piercing plus resonating weaponry **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 30, attack +22 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Summon Instrument](/Spells.aspx?ID=1703), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **4th** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (3 slots) - **5th** [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) (3 slots) - **6th** [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685), [Vibrant Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741) (2 slots) **Bard Composition Spells** DC 30, 1 Focus Point - **Cantrips (6th)** [Courageous Anthem](/Spells.aspx?ID=1763), [Dirge of Doom](/Spells.aspx?ID=1764) - **6th** [Counter Performance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1762) **Resonating Weaponry** The maestro funnels musical energy from their compositions into attacks, dealing additional 1d6 sonic damage with their weapon Strikes on any turn they cast a [composition](/Traits.aspx?ID=559) spell.
name: Maestro

<title level="1" pfs="">[Druid Initiate](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3580)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A neophyte druid learns basic techniques and spellcasting soon after being initiated into their druidic order. The druid initiate has already learned much from their mentors—elders among the order—but hasn't had a chance to develop their own identity.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Druid_Initiate.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Druid Initiate](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3580)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 547

**Perception** +7

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Wildsong](/Languages.aspx?ID=116)

**Skills**
[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +3, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +7, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +4, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Healer's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Primal Symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2743), [Sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72) (10 bullets), [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Ref** +4 

**Will** +9

</row>

</column>

---

[Cornucopia](/Spells.aspx?ID=1856)

<title level="1" pfs="">[Pack Leader](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3581)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Primal spellcasters with a particular affinity for animals often aid their community by training and healing their domesticated animals. Others focus their time on preserving endangered species and helping them propagate or adapt to a changing environment. The pack leader is most often paired with the [trained bat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3676) companion; see the Pack Leader Companions sidebar for other options.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Pack_Leader.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Pack Leader](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3581)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 132

**Perception** +12

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Wildsong](/Languages.aspx?ID=116)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +6, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +9, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Animal Empathy** The pack leader can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill with [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531).

**Items**
[Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=9), [Sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72) (10 bullets)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 55

</row>

**Stay Strong!** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** An allied [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) within 30 feet attempts a saving throw; **Effect** The pack leader shouts a word of encouragement, granting the allied animal a +1 circumstance bonus to the save.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sickle +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sling +11 ([Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 bludgeoning

**Primal Prepared Spells** DC 20, attack +12
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Gouging Claw](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713)
- **1st**
[Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Pet Cache](/Spells.aspx?ID=1627)
- **2nd**
[Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=1441), [Enlarge](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514), [Summon Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1694)

**Druid Order Focus Spells** DC 20, 1 Focus Point
- **2nd**
[Heal Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1855)

**Timely Trick** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The pack leader commands an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) ally within 30 feet to perform a specific action; the target can spend its reaction to immediately Step, Stride, or Strike.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Pack Leader Companions</title>

In addition to the [trained bat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3676) companion (and, optionally, with one of the [support benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=3381)), these _Monster Core_ animals make good companions for the pack leader. **Level 3** [dire wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3242), [giant mantis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3094), [giant scorpion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3175), [giant wasp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3233), [gorilla](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2827), [grizzly bear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2850), [hyaenodon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3066), [lion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2866), [pachycephalosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2917); **Level 4** [daeodon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2855), [giant stag beetle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2853), [great white shark](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3188), [griffon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3034), [hadrosaurid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2918), [rhinoceros](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3168), [tiger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2867).
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-547" />
name: Pack Leader

<title level="1" pfs="">[Mountain Guardian](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3582)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Those who have unlocked the secrets of opening a kinetic gate to an elemental plane within themselves wield immense power over that element. Practitioners who specialize in elemental earth are hardy and defense-minded. The mountain guardian is surrounded by heavy armor made of stone held together with elemental magic.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Mountain_Guardian.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Mountain Guardian](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3582)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 133

**Perception** +9; [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) 10 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +1

**Con** +4

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +11 (+13 vs. damaging effects)

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 100

</row>

**Resistances**
[earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) 6, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 6

**Kinetic Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 10 feet. Pieces of rock and earth float in the aura. The aura must be active for the guardian to use [impulse](/Traits.aspx?ID=502) actions, and deactivates if the guardian uses an [overflow](/Traits.aspx?ID=506) impulse, is [knocked out](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or Dismisses it. The guardian can Channel Elements to reactivate it.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
elemental blast +16 ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [Impulse](/Traits.aspx?ID=502), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 2d8+4 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
elemental blast +16 ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [Impulse](/Traits.aspx?ID=502), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 2d8 bludgeoning

**Base Kinesis** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The mountain guardian generates, moves, or suppresses up to 1 Bulk of naturally occurring earthen matter within 15 feet. Generating creates earthen matter, moving moves existing matter up to 20 feet into any direction, and suppressing destroys a piece of that element (which can't be a durable crafted good, only natural forms of the element).

**Channel Elements** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The mountain guardian reactivates their kinetic aura and can make an elemental blast Strike.

**Empowered Blast** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The mountain guardian makes a melee or ranged elemental blast Strike with a +4 status bonus to damage.

**Tremor** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [Impulse](/Traits.aspx?ID=502), [Overflow](/Traits.aspx?ID=506), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The mountain guardian stomps on natural earth or stone, causing a localized tremor. All creatures in a 10-foot burst within 30 feet take 3d10 bludgeoning damage with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature hat critically fails is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). Earth and stone in the area is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) until the start of the mountain guardian's next turn.

**Weight of Stone** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [Impulse](/Traits.aspx?ID=502), [Overflow](/Traits.aspx?ID=506), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The mountain guardian calls down boulders in a cylinder 20 feet in diameter and 80 feet high within 120 feet. Each creature in the area takes 4d8 bludgeoning damage with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that fails is also [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) downward 40 feet (80 feet on a critical failure) without taking [falling damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) and can't leave the ground for 1 round.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Kineticist Rules</title>

The mountain guardian is based on the [kineticist](/Classes.aspx?ID=23) class from _Pathfinder Rage of Elements_, though simplified for use and an NPC. Their actions with the [impulse](/Traits.aspx?ID=502) trait can be used only if their kinetic aura is active and they have a hand free. When they use an action with the [overflow](/Traits.aspx?ID=506) trait (Tremor or Weight of Stone), their kinetic aura deactivates until they Channel Elements.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-547" />
name: Mountain Guardian

<title level="1" pfs="">[Dedicated Druid](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3583)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Individuals who dedicate their life to the protection and preservation of the natural world often become druids. These devoted practitioners of primal magic might work alone or in a circle of like-minded individuals, all of whom have primal powers more terrifying than the last.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Dedicated_Druid.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Dedicated Druid](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3583)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 134

**Perception** +15; [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Wildsong](/Languages.aspx?ID=116)

**Skills**
[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +2

**Con** +1

**Int** +1

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Plant Empathy** The dedicated druid can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill with [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) and [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614).

**Items**
[Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7), [_scroll of revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) (2), _+1 [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=365)_, [Wooden Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=2) (Hardness 3, HP 12, BT 6)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 100

</row>

**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_spear_ +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 1d6+8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_spear_ +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d6+8 piercing

**Primal Prepared Spells** DC 25, attack +17
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713), [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744)
- **1st**
[Air Bubble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1438), [Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550)
- **2nd**
[Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515), [Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), [One with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618)
- **3rd**
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752)
- **4th**
[Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586)

**Druid Order Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 25
- **4th**
[Cornucopia](/Spells.aspx?ID=1856)

**Nature's Patient Healing** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Requirement** The dedicated druid is in a natural environment; **Effect** The dedicated druid camouflages themself to blend in with the surrounding area, sprouting leaves or covering themself with scree. They gain [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) until the end of their next turn, they can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) with a +4 circumstance bonus, and they recover 4d8 Hit Points. If the druid moves or otherwise leaves their space, these benefits end.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-547" />
name: Dedicated Druid

Skin shifters are defenders of nature, blessed by the spirits of the wild with the ability to shape shift into powerful animal forms. Prowling as beasts or charging across the field, they protect the balance of nature and punish those who would upset it. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Skin Shifter](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3584) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 134 **Perception** +16; (18 in animal form) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Wildsong](/Languages.aspx?ID=116) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +11, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +18 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +1 **Animal Empathy** The skin shifter can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill with [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531). **Items** [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=436)_ (20 arrows), [Spiked Gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=11) --- **AC** 25 (26 in animal form) **Fort** +15 **Ref** +14 **Will** +16 **HP** 140 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** spiked gauntlet +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Free-Hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=613)), **Damage** 1d4+10 piercing **Ranged** _longbow_ +17 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 2d8+6 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 26 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Gouging Claw](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) **Gift of the Wild Spirits** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The skin shifter casts their choice of a 4th-rank [_aerial form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1437), [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1440), [_dinosaur form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1489), or [_pest form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1626) spell. They must transform into an animal of a kind they've seen within the last 24 hours. They can't gain temporary HP again from a spell cast with Gift of the Wild Spirits for 10 minutes. Their Strikes for forms other than _pest form_ have [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) 10 feet, a +20 attack modifier, and a +13 damage bonus (or a +9 damage bonus for aerial form). Most other changes to their statistics are listed above.
While polymorphed, the skin shifter can still use Gift of the Wild Spirits, though they're still prevented from casting other spells as normal.
name: Skin Shifter

Singers can speak the words of creation in song, a forgotten art from the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35). Their melodies turn grass into blades and make trees stir. Through their music, plants rise to defend and seek vengeance for nature. Taught by woodland spirits or [fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) agents of the First World, they master the magic of primal song. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Tree Singer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3585) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 135 **Perception** +22 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Wildsong](/Languages.aspx?ID=116) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +23, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +26, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +22 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=361)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=434)_ --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +21 **Will** +25 **HP** 220 **Bloodthirsty Plants** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** An enemy in the tree singer's Verdant Aria aura (see below) attacks one of the tree singer's allies; **Effect** Vines and branches to lash out at the attacker, dealing 3d6 piercing damage.
**Plant Empathy** The tree singer can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill with [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) and [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _longspear_ +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+10 piercing plus 2d10 sonic **Melee** fist +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning plus 2d10 sonic **Ranged** _composite longbow_ +23 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 2d8+8 piercing plus 1d10 sonic **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 33, attack +25 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) - **1st** [Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542) (×2), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) - **2nd** [Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515), [One with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) (×2) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506) (×2), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677) - **4th** [Oaken Resilience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1617), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) - **5th** [Elemental Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1510) (wood only), [Nature's Pathway](/Spells.aspx?ID=1613), [Plant Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1637) - **6th** [Plant Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1637), [Tangling Creepers](/Spells.aspx?ID=1714), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752) - **7th** [Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1648), [Tree of Seasons](/Spells.aspx?ID=1725) **Druid Order Spells** DC 33, 1 Focus Point - **7th** [Cornucopia](/Spells.aspx?ID=1856) **Verdant Aria** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) The tree singer raises their voice in a haunting melody, creating an aura in a 30-foot emanation. Plants in the aura seem to come to life, swaying and rustling in response to the music. The tree singer's allies in the aura gain a +2 status bonus to AC and saving throws as the foliage around them shields and defends them from harm. The aura lasts until the end of the tree singer's next turn but can be Sustained. It can be Sustained even if the tree singer is [polymorphed](/Traits.aspx?ID=670). The effect ends early if the tree singer stops singing.
name: Tree Singer
