<title level="1" pfs="">[Interrogator](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3615)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Interrogators use pain and intimidation against prisoners and other helpless victims to force “confessions.”

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Interrogator.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Interrogator](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3615)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 158

**Perception** +13

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Items**
[Dart](/Weapons.aspx?ID=68) (5), [Healer's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727), leather apron (functions as [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)), _+1 [war razor](/Weapons.aspx?ID=307)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 22 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_war razor_ +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 1d4+10 slashing plus torment

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning plus torment

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dart +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d4+7 piercing plus torment

**Blood and Fear** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The interrogator Strikes with a slashing melee weapon. If they hit and deal damage, the target takes an additional 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (or 2d4 persistent bleed damage and frightened 2 on a critical hit). Each of the interrogator's other enemies in a 30-foot emanation around the target that witnesses the bloodshed must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or be frightened 1. The frightened part of this ability is an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effect.

**Hobble** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** A creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by the interrogator; **Effect** One creature grabbed or restrained by the interrogator takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. If the creature fails its save, it also gains a condition of the interrogator's choice: [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) for 1 minute, [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) for 1 minute, or [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68).

**Torment**   The interrogator's Strikes deal an additional 1d8 mental damage to [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) creatures.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-550" />
name: Interrogator

markdown:
It's the reckless scientist's job to break the rules of reality, no matter the cost. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Reckless Scientist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3616) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 158 **Perception** +12 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +9, [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 **Unstable Collection** A reckless scientist carries a collection of poorly stowed alchemical items: 3 [elixirs of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308) and 6 alchemical grenades. The scientist replenishes these items each day using scavenged materials. The alchemical grenades deal either acid, cold, or fire damage plus 2 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and 2 [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage of the same type (typically the collection contains two of each grenade). **Items** [Alchemist's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2702), _+1 [sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364)_, work coat (functions as [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)) --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +14 **Will** +10 +1 status to all saves vs. [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **HP** 95 **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 5 **Unstable Explosion** When an attacker scores a critical hit against the reckless scientist, one of the scientist's alchemical items bursts. The GM determines the item randomly. If it was a bomb, the alchemist takes damage from the bomb, and any creature adjacent to the alchemist takes the splash damage. Any other item is wasted. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** sickle +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 1d4+7 slashing **Melee** fist +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning **Ranged** alchemical grenade +16 ([range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699)), **Damage** 2d6 acid, cold, or fire plus 2 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and 2 [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage of the same type **Quick Grenadier** The reckless scientist Interacts to draw an alchemical grenade with an Interact action and throws it as a ranged Strike. **Reckless Alchemy** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The reckless scientist attempts to combine two alchemical grenades or two elixirs of life into one item. They can Interact to draw the items if necessary. They attempt a DC 22 Crafting check, destroying both component items to create one new item.
**Success** The new item has the full effect of both component items, and the reckless scientist can Activate it. If they don't Activate it before the end of their turn, the item explodes (as critical failure).
**Failure** The new item is inert.
**Critical Failure** The unstable item explodes, dealing 3d6 piercing damage to the reckless scientist
name: Reckless Scientist

markdown:
Cruel scientists called fleshwarpers create horrors from the flesh of others. Many desire to push science forward, but others need only grotesque glee. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Fleshwarper](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3617) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 159 **Perception** +13 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Aberration Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +17, [Fleshwarping Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +17, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 **Items** fleshwarping concoction, [Healer's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727), _+1 scalpel_ (functions as a [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)) --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +15 **Will** +15 **HP** 110 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _scalpel_ +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+9 piercing **Melee** fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+9 bludgeoning **Ranged** fleshwarping concoction +17 ([Alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=528), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d6 poison plus flesh mutation **Ranged** _scalpel_ +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+9 piercing **Conduct the Experiment** The fleshwarper assesses vulnerabilities in a creature's anatomy. They attempt a [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) check against the Fortitude DC of one living creature they can see within 60 feet. On a success, the fleshwarper's melee Strikes deal an extra 2d8 precision damage against that creature for 1 minute or until the fleshwarper critically hits that creature, whichever comes first. Using this action again designates a new target and ends the effect for any previous target. A fleshwarper can target an individual no more than once per day with this ability. **Flesh Mutation** ([Alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=528), [Morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657)) A creature made of flesh that's hit by a fleshwarping concoction Strike is subject to a random fleshwarping mutation determined by rolling 1d4 and consulting the list below. The creature attempts a DC 25 Fortitude save at the end of each of its turns, ending the mutation on a success. A creature that becomes mutated is thereafter temporarily immune to flesh mutation for 1 day.
  • **Spongy Flesh** The creature has weakness 5 to physical damage.
  • **Caustic Blood** The creature takes 2d4 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) that can't be removed normally, but ends when the mutation does.
  • **Sprouting Eyes** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65), but also immune to flanking.
  • **Mutated Mind** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63). It can still recover as noted in the condition, but if it does it remains [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the mutation ends.
**Restore My Masterpiece** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The fleshwarper is holding or wearing a healer's toolkit; **Effect** The fleshwarper stitches the wounds of an adjacent, willing aberration or creature they modified using fleshwarping. The creature regains 20 HP and is then temporarily immune for 1 day.
Warped Menagerie A fleshwarper is a master at their dark craft, and creating monsters is practically second nature to them. However, they have no great skill at controlling monsters. Some may keep their creations caged for further study, or to use as grim guard dogs. The especially careless may simply abandon their awful abominations in the wild to become someone else's problem. Classically, their creations are fleshwarps like the [grothlut](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2997) and [irnakurse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2998). Other created creatures might resemble [charnel creations](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2878), [globsters](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3019), or [sinspawn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3192).
name: Fleshwarper

Gang leaders direct cutthroats, killers, thieves, and toughs. The gang leader often appears alongside a [bandit gang](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3432) or other criminals. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Gang Leader](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3618) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 160 **Perception** +14 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +15, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** -1 **Cha** +4 **Items** [moderate glue bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3295), [lesser healing potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=186), _+1 [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398)_, [Sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72) (10 bullets), [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5) --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +17 **Will** +12 **HP** 110 **Deny Advantage** The gang leader isn't [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to creatures of 7th level or lower that are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), flanking, or using surprise attack.
**Evasive Reflexes** When the gang leader rolls a success on a Reflex save, they get a critical success instead.
**Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** The gang leader is targeted with an attack by an attacker they can see; **Effect** The gang leader gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning **Melee** _shortsword_ +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+10 piercing **Ranged** sling +17 ([Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+8 bludgeoning **Brutal Rally** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** The gang leader rolls a critical hit against a creature; **Effect** All allies that can see the gang leader gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls until the start of the gang leader's next turn. **Gang Up** Any enemy is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the gang leader's melee attacks due to flanking as long as the enemy is within melee reach of both the gang leader and one of the gang leader's allies. **Quick Draw** The gang leader Interacts to draw a weapon, then Strikes with that weapon. **Sneak Attack** The gang leader deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. **Surprise Attacker** On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the gang leader.
Gang Structure A gang leader might run a gang, and several other NPCs in this section and the [Criminal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=533) section make for good gang members. A gang of significant size typically has a pyramid structure so that only a few members report directly to the boss and it's harder to link crimes directly to those in charge if someone gets arrested.
name: Gang Leader

By night, this member of the nobility dons a false identity to mete out violent, extralegal justice on petty criminals and the downtrodden. They're possessed of unwavering self-righteousness and the best equipment money can buy. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Wealthy Vigilante](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3619) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 160 **Perception** +15 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 **Custom Gear** The wealthy vigilante's support team has spent years tailoring and tuning the vigilante's equipment. Anyone but the vigilante attempting to use the items takes the same drawbacks they would if they were [shoddy items](/Rules.aspx?ID=2162). These peculiarities make the items have no value if sold.
**Talisman Prepper** The vigilante goes on patrol with six talismans of 6th level or lower. The typical set includes a [_fear gem_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2975) and [_emerald grasshopper_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2972) affixed, with a [_dragon turtle scale_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2970), [_effervescent ampoule_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2971), [_feather step stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2976), and [_iron cube_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2980) in storage. **Items** _crimefighting pouches_ (function as [_sleeves of storage_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3110)), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [flintlock musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=521)_ (10 rounds), [_lifting belt_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3091), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_, [_moderate healing potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2943), [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +17 **Will** +15 **HP** 120 **Quick Replace** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Trigger** The wealthy vigilante Activates one of their affixed talismans; **Requirements** The wealthy vigilante has a hand free; **Effect** As soon as one of their talismans burns out, the wealthy vigilante pulls another from their _crimefighting pouches_ and deftly [Affixes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2632) it to replace the used talisman. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _longsword_ +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing **Melee** fist +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning **Ranged** _flintlock musket_ +17 ([Concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=846), [fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 70 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d6+6 piercing **Calculated Strike** The wealthy vigilante makes a melee Strike. If the Strike hits, the vigilante can then [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) the target. This Shove uses the same multiple attack penalty as the Strike and doesn't count toward the vigilante's multiple attack penalty, but the vigilante must Stride after the pushed creature. If the Strike misses, the vigilante can Step up to three times, each of which must take it further from the target. The vigilante can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) if, after the Steps, they have [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) or [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from the target.
Vigilante's Arsenal A wealthy vigilante's threat (and flexibility) as a combatant comes from their ability to use the right gear at the right time. A canny vigilante enters combat with the best consumables readied and talismans affixed for the type of encounter to come, along with a plan for what to use next. As a GM, you can swap out the wealthy vigilante's talismans over multiple engagements to make them appear much more threatening than they are. Keep the PCs guessing!
name: Wealthy Vigilante

Warmongers believe the base state of life is violence. They stay in peak physical condition with constant training and keep their supplies ready for marching to war. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Warmonger](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3620) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 161 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +14, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +21 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +1 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +0 **War Ready** The warmonger can always roll [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) for initiative. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [battle axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=371)_ (2), _+1 [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=434)_ (10 arrows), _+1 [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44)_ --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +20 **Will** +16 **HP** 200 **Pain Training** The warmonger treats the value of any [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) conditions affecting them as 1 lower. The warmonger still has the condition and must remove it normally.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 10 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** _battle axe_ +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d8+12 slashing **Melee** fist +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+12 bludgeoning **Ranged** _composite longbow_ +21 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 1d8+9 piercing **Patch and Set** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The warmonger has a hand free; **Effect** The warmonger grits their teeth and ties off a wound or sets a bone or joint. They regain 20 Hit Points. **Power Through** **Requirements** The warmonger is wielding two melee weapons and isn't [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73); **Effect** The warmonger attempts up to three melee Strikes against different creatures. These count toward the warmonger's multiple attack penalty normally, but the penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks. The warmonger overexerts themself with the attacks, becoming fatigued. The warmonger can attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save to recover from this fatigued condition at the start of each of their turns. **Sight Prey** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The warmonger singles out one enemy to bring down with ranged attacks until the end of the current turn. The warmonger's ranged Strikes against that creature gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the attack roll and deal an extra 3d6 precision damage. Each time the warmonger hits that creature with a ranged Strike, the creature takes a –10-foot penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute and [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) 20 feet if it's flying. **War Cry** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** The warmonger critically hits or knocks out an enemy; **Effect** The warmonger screams a battle cry. Each ally in a 30-foot emanation that hears it deals an additional 1d6 damage with its Strikes for 1 round.
name: Warmonger

Some hunters grow bored of simple beasts and monsters. For them, a battle-tested warrior is the finest prey. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Hero Hunter](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3621) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 162 **Perception** +25 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +26, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +19, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +25 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Prepared Trapper** A hero hunter carries the materials to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) two [alarm snares](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3363), two [grasping snares](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3372), one [snagging hook snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3384), and one [stunning snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3388). The hero hunter replenishes any used supplies each time they make their daily preparations. Snare rules can be found [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3315). **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=376)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [hand crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=427)_ (20 bolts), _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=42)_ --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +25 **Will** +21 **HP** 230 **Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** The hero hunter is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker they can see **Effect** The hero hunter gains a +2 circumstance
bonus to AC against the triggering attack.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 10 feet **Melee** _greataxe_ +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d12+13 slashing plus hunter's precision **Melee** fist +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+13 bludgeoning plus hunter's precision **Ranged** _hand crossbow_ +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d6+8 piercing plus hunter's precision **Deadly Snares** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The hero hunter [Crafts](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) a snare that would normally take 1 minute or less to Craft. The [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) DC to locate the snare and DC to disable it with [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) are equal to the hero hunter's [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) DC if it's higher than the snare's DC. **Felling Shot** The hero hunter makes a ranged Strike. If it hits and deals damage to a flying target, the target [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) up to 120 feet but takes no damage from the fall. The creature can't [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), Leap, levitate or otherwise leave the ground until the end of the hero hunter's next turn. **Hunter's Precision** ([Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=701)) The hero hunter knows how to hunt and kill any game. While in this stance, all the hero hunter's Strikes deal an additional 2d8 precision damage, and the range increment for their ranged weapon Strikes is 20 feet longer than normal. If the hunter gets a critical hit with a weapon Strike, the target also takes 2d6 persistent bleed damage.
Home Advantage A cocky hero hunter may attack the heroes on their own turf, simply trusting in their skills to win the battle. However, their best move is to lure the PCs to a battlefield they are familiar with, where they've had time to set up their traps. A hero hunter can choose an advantageous place to set their ambush and place snares. They rarely call in allies to assist them unless it's to keep their prey from escaping.
name: Hero Hunter

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[World Ender](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3622)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Unlike most villains, world enders are unburdened by nuance. Their ultimate goal is simple, if lofty: destroy the world and everyone in it.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 40

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/World_Ender.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 16">[World Ender](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3622)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 36 

**Fort** +30 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +28 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 275

</row>

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 15

**Unyielding Purpose** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The world ender would be reduced to 0 HP; **Requirements** The world ender has a [_volcanic eruption_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1746) spell remaining; **Effect** The world ender refuses to let their destructive dream go unrealized, stabilizing at 1 HP just long enough to cast _volcanic eruption_, centered on themself. They die, immolated in the eruption.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_staff of fire_ +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)),
**Damage** 2d4+10 bludgeoning plus 3d6 fire

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning plus 3d6 fire

**Primal Prepared Spells** DC 41, attack +33
- **Cantrips (8th)**
[Caustic Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1461), [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [Gouging Claw](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585)
- **1st**
[Air Bubble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1438), [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550)
- **2nd**
[Darkvision](/Spells.aspx?ID=1481), [Enlarge](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653), [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756)
- **3rd**
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506) (×2), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677)
- **4th**
[Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) (×2), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) (×2)
- **5th**
[Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) (×2), [Magic Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1591), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751)
- **6th**
[Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462) (×2), [Floating Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=1533), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748)
- **7th**
[Blazing Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450), [Fiery Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=1527), [Volcanic Eruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=1746) (×2)
- **8th**
[Desiccate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1484), [Punishing Winds](/Spells.aspx?ID=1643), [Sunburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1707) (×2)

**Monologue** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Throughout combat, the world ender ceaselessly expounds upon the righteous reasons for their destructive aims and the futility of their enemies' efforts to stop them. They gain a +1 status bonus to Will saves and a +2 status bonus to damage rolls with their spells. Each time they take this action again, the bonuses increase by 1 and 2, respectively. The monologue ends (and the bonuses are lost) if the world ender becomes unable to act or speak, or if they end their turn without having taken this action.

**Overwhelming Energy** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) If the next action the world ender uses is to Cast a Spell, the spell ignores 20 resistance to energy damage. This applies to all damage the spell deals, including [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and damage caused by an ongoing effect of the spell. A creature's immunities are unaffected.

</column>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Some catfolk take advantage of their natural agility to steal from those too slow to catch them. They usually target notorious misers and others who obviously have money to spare—sometimes to help the needy, sometimes for simple profit.

**Stealthy Pad** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The leaping thief Steps, then [Hides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneaks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), ignoring [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for this movement.

**Unexpected Angle**   When the leaping thief successfully [Tumbles Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370) a foe's space or [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) to a position higher than a foe, the foe is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the next attack the leaping thief makes before the end of their turn.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Lucky Courser](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3625)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Masterful catfolk hunters travel the far corners of the world, stalking terrible the monsters who dwell there in hopes of someday putting an end to their predations. They eagerly cooperate with other adventurers to defeat [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387), [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=523), and malevolent beasts and [fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119).

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<traits>

<trait label="Catfolk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=746" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +18; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Amurrun](/Languages.aspx?ID=25), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +14, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +16, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Warning Ears** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Trigger** The lucky courser rolls initiative using Perception or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49); **Effect** Their expressive ears twitch in alarm, granting allies within 10 feet a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [arbalest](/Weapons.aspx?ID=432)_ (20 [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) bolts, 20 [dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=377) bolts), +1 whip, [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

**Ref** +18 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 140

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_whip_ +20 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),

**Damage** 2d10+6 piercing

**Feline Skirmish** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The lucky courser can Interact to reload their arbalest, Step, and Strike, taking the actions in any order. The Step ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).

**Head Shot** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The lucky courser [Creates a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387) and then Strikes. The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of the lucky courser's next turn on a successful Strike (or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) on a critical hit).

</column>

markdown:
Many dwarves become smiths as their attention to detail, lifestyles that keep them close to useful materials such as iron, and a pride in their work all come together to become a brilliant skill set for working with armaments. Though dwarves are capable of smithing most any kind of item, most focus on armaments as a way of creating objects to defend their fellow dwarves. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Dwarf Smith](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3626) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 174 **Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +6, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +12, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +6 **Str** +2 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** +3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -1 **Blacksmithing Specialist** For encounters involving blacksmithing, the dwarf smith is a 5th-level challenge.
**Temper Armament** ([downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580)) The smith spends 1 day tempering a single suit of metallic armor, metallic shield, or metallic weapon. Tempering armor or a shield increases its Hardness by 1. Tempering a weapon grants the weapon a +1 circumstance bonus to damage rolls. Regardless of the item, the tempering remains for 3 days, after which item is temporarily immune to further tempering for 1 week as the technique would otherwise damage it. **Items** [Artisan's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703) (blacksmithing), [Clan Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=13), leather apron (functions as [padded armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=40)), [Light Hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=384) --- **AC** 14 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +3 **Will** +5 **HP** 12 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 1 --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** light hammer +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d6+2 bludgeoning **Melee** clan dagger +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+2 piercing **Melee** fist +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning **Ranged** light hammer +4 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6+2 bludgeoning **Crack the Shell** The dwarf smith makes a Strike to break a creature's defenses. If the Strike hits and the creature is wearing armor with Hardness 9 or lower, the armor is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60). This Strike doesn't further damage armor that's already broken.
name: Dwarf Smith

<title level="1" pfs="">[Spirit Priest](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3627)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

While dwarves worship gods like most any other ancestry, many dwarves also look to the spirits of the world for guidance and support. Most every object and environment in the world, from a simple rock to an expansive river to the largest of mountains, contains some kind of spirit. Dwarven spirit priests learn how to communicate with these spirits. This spiritual attunement allows these priests to use magic, as well as call on these spirits in times of need.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Spirit_Priest.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Spirit Priest](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3627)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=584" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 174

**Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Dwarf Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +10, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +0

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Items**
[Clan Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 78

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
clan dagger +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning

**Divine Spontaneous Spells** DC 22, attack +14
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689)
- **1st**
[Bless](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Infuse Vitality](/Spells.aspx?ID=1574), [Spirit Link](/Spells.aspx?ID=1686) (4 slots)
- **2nd**
[Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) (4 slots)
- **3rd**
[Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), [Safe Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659), [Spiritual Armament](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687) (3 slots)

**Spirit's Interference** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The spirit priest calls out to a local spirit to assault the priest's enemies. The spirit unleashes a blast of rocks, attacks with a set of vines, or uses some other appropriate part of the environment to attack all creatures in a 10-foot burst within 30 feet of the priest. The attack deals 6d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The spirit priest can't use Spirit's Interference for 1d4 rounds. The GM might have this ability deal a different damage type based on the local spirits, such as fire damage when calling on a fire spirit.

**Spiritual Edge** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513), [Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) The spirit priest aligns their spirit with their magical effects, enhancing the power of their spells. If their next action is to Cast a Spell that deals damage and doesn't have a duration, the spell deals additional spirit damage equal to the spell's rank.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-405" />
name: Spirit Priest

Dwarven soldiers make formidable units, capable of holding their own against most enemies. In combat, a dwarf battalion is a well-oiled machine, unleashing coordinated attacks against foes. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Dwarf Battalion](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3628) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 175 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +13, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11 **Str** +5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +11 **Will** +13 **HP** 105 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **Dwarven Doughtiness** Dwarves are often calm and collected in the face of imminent danger. At the end of the battalion's turn, reduce its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition by 2 instead of 1.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)**
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** --- **Speed** 20 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Bombing Barrage** The dwarf battalion draws alchemical bombs, then hurls them at distant foes. This volley is a 10-foot burst within 60 feet that deals 3d6 damage with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage is either acid, fire, or electricity damage, depending on which type of bombs the battalion used. When the dwarf battalion is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Coordinated Pummel** Frequency once per round; Effect The dwarf battalion unleashes a storm of warhammer blows against each enemy in a 5-foot emanation (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8 bludgeoning damage
2d8+5 bludgeoning damage
3d8+5 bludgeoning damage **Dwarven War Song** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The battalion joins together to sing a traditional song of battle. Each enemy in a 30-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 23 Will save or be [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (or frightened 2 on a critical failure). Each enemy is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes. **Shields Up!** The battalion raises their steel shields. It gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and Reflex saves until the start of its next turn.
name: Dwarf Battalion

Dwarven generals embody pride in knowledge and tactical acumen, using their understanding of warfare and battlefield strategy to coordinate their subordinates into optimal locations. They also remain ready to enter combat at a moment's notice and bring a fight to an enemy themselves. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Dwarf General](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3629) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 176 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +15, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15 **Str** +5 **Dex** +0 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Opening Orders** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [linguistic [free-action]](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) **Trigger** The dwarf general rolls initiative and can see at least one enemy; **Effect** The general unleashes a command to ready for combat. Each ally within 120 feet that can hear the general can either [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316) or Step as a free action when it rolls initiative. **Items** [Arbalest](/Weapons.aspx?ID=432) (10 bolts), [Clan Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=13), [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=403)_ --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +14 **Will** +16 **HP** 150 **Dwarven Doughtiness** Dwarves are often calm and collected in the face of imminent danger. At the end of the general's turn, reduce its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition by 2 instead of 1.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** The dwarf general gains an additional reaction at the
beginning of each of their turns that they can use only for a Reactive Strike.
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _warhammer_ +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 2d8+11 bludgeoning **Melee** clan dagger +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+11 piercing **Melee** fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+11 bludgeoning **Ranged** arbalest +15 ([Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [range increment 110 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d10+6 piercing **Advancing Orders** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) The dwarf general issues a command to push forward on the battlefield. Each ally who hears and understands this command becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) until the end of its next turn but can use the extra action only to Step or Stride. **Hammer Critical Specialization** When the general critically hits with a [hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8), the target of the critical hit is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) unless it succeeds at a DC 26 Fortitude save. **Sudden Charge** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The dwarf general Strides twice. If they end their movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, they can make a melee Strike against that enemy.
name: Dwarf General

Many societies recognize aiuvarins' skills as adept translators. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Aiuvarin Translator](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3630) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 178 **Perception** +5; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4); two other common or [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=721) languages **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +7, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +8, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +7, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +6, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +5, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +7 **Str** +0 **Dex** +2 **Con** +0 **Int** +3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 **Linguistic Mastery** The translator gains a +5 circumstance bonus to skill checks involving translating or deciphering languages. If the translator rolls a critical failure on a check to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2363), they get a failure instead.
**Translation Specialist** For encounters involving translating or deciphering languages, the translator is a 4th-level challenge. **Items** book of translations, quill pen (functions as a [dart](/Weapons.aspx?ID=426)), [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12), [Writing Set](/Equipment.aspx?ID=63) --- **AC** 14 **Fort** +2 **Ref** +6 **Will** +9 **HP** 12 **Crosstalk** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature within 20 feet of the translator would be targeted by or in the area of an ability with the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) trait; **Effect** The translator attempts a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check with a +5 circumstance bonus against the Will DC of the creature. On a success, the creature is unaffected by the linguistic effect, and the translator can choose to make the creature [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) until the end of the creature's next turn. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** staff +4 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning **Ranged** quill pen +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d4+2 piercing

<title level="1" pfs="">[Forlorn Artist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3631)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Forlorn elves, who spend their lives among shorter-lived peoples, often grow morose from constant loss. Some channel this melancholy into their art.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Forlorn Artist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3631)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Elf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=588" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 178

**Perception** +7; (9 to notice unusual artwork) [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4); one regional language

**Skills**
[Art Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +9, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** -1

**Int** +4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Art Specialist** For encounters involving crafting or evaluating art, the forlorn artist is a 4th-level challenge.

**Items**
art supplies, [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +3 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +9 

+1 circumstance vs. [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effects
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 26

</row>

**Flick Ink** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The artist is targeted with a melee or ranged Strike by a creature within 15 feet; **Effect** The artist flings ink in the attacker's eyes. The attacker must succeed at a DC 18 Reflex save or be [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59). This takes effect before the attacker targets the artist. The blindness lasts until the end of the target's next turn, but the creature can Interact to rub its eyes to attempt a new save to end the condition.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rapier +9 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 18
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673)

**Cry of Ages** <actions string="Single Action" />  The artist channels their loneliness into a wordless wail that forces others to contemplate their mortality. Each enemy in a 30-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 17 Will save or be [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). A creature that critically fails is also [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 minute. Each creature is then temporarily immune for 1 minute.

</column>

name: Forlorn Artist

Forlorn elves, who spend their lives among shorter-lived peoples, often grow morose from constant loss. Some channel this melancholy into their art.
</summary>

summary_markdown: Forlorn elves, who spend their lives among shorter-lived peoples, often grow morose from constant loss. Some channel this melancholy into their art.

Elves of the forest or jungle take advantage of the terrain, operating in units that seemingly appear from the trees themselves. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Woodland Scouts](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3633) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 179 **Perception** +18; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Forest Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +17, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +14, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +16 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +18 **Will** +16 **HP** 120 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, splash damage 8 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 30 feet; forest passage, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Among the Trees** **Requirements** Every square the woodland scouts occupy is in forest terrain; **Effect** The woodland scouts disperse among the trees. They [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) and then use that cover to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404), attempting a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) check. **Forest Passage** Woodland scouts ignore any [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) caused by plants and fungi, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth. **Longbow Barrage** The scouts draw or reload their longbows, then send forth a flurry of arrows. This barrage is a 10-foot burst within 100 feet that deals 3d8 piercing damage with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. If the scouts are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), this deals an additional 2d6 precision damage. When the troop is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Thicket of Blades** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The scouts engage in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+3 slashing damage
2d8+6 slashing damage
3d8+9 slashing damage **Stealthy Formation** If the scouts become [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), they remain so until they take a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300).
name: Woodland Scouts

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In the tangled web of lineages, rivalries, and shifting alliances that is an elven noble court, aristocratic families employ bodyguards loyal to them alone.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 31

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Elf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=588" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 180

**Perception** +24; plus vigilance

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); plus one regional language

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24, [Heraldry Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +19

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +5

**Con** +2

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Vigilance** A court guard gains a +1 circumstance bonus on Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) and [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) creatures, and if they succeed, they get a critical success instead.

**Items**
_+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=43)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [low-grade cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) [elven curve blade](/Weapons.aspx?ID=406)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=434)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 35 

**Fort** +20 

**Ref** +27 

**Will** +23 

+1 status vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 225

</row>

**Interposition** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** An ally within 15 feet of the guard would take damage; **Effect** The guard Strides. This movement does not trigger reactions, and the guard must end the Stride in a space adjacent to the ally. The guard then switches places with the ally, taking all damage and associated effects instead of the ally.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_elven curve blade_ +27 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d8+12 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+12 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_composite longbow_ +27 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)),
**Damage** 2d8+10 piercing

**Avenge the Fallen** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The guard is within 30 feet of the creature they were guarding, and that creature is either [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) or died since the guard's last turn; **Effect** The guard Strikes the creature that damaged their ally. They roll the attack roll twice and use the higher result.

**Dancing Blade** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The guard makes a Strike against a creature, then Strides. This Stride doesn't trigger reactions. If the guard ends this Stride in a different space adjacent to the same creature, they make another Strike against it. If both Strikes succeed, the creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of the guard's next turn. Each attack counts toward the guard's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until they've made both attacks.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-414" />
name: Elven Court Guard

The innate gnomish inclination to revel in new experiences leads gnome philomaths to delve as deeply as they can into the multitudes of knowledge contained within a library. These shrewd observers have encyclopedic memories and tend to surround themselves with piles of documents and trinkets pertaining to whatever subject currently holds their attention. As their interests branch from one topic to the next, a gnome philomath files away their discoveries to be referenced again another day. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 13 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8 [Gnome Philomath](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3635) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 182 **Perception** +5; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +3, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +1, [History Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +5, [One Additional Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +5, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +4, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +3 **Str** +0 **Dex** +1 **Con** +0 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Local Records Specialist** For encounters involving local records and histories, the gnome philomath is a 5th-level challenge.
**Helpful Hoard** Gnome philomaths can quickly find almost any document in their vast collection. They gain a +8 circumstance bonus to skill checks involving local records and histories. **Items** [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12), [Writing Set](/Equipment.aspx?ID=63) --- **AC** 12 **Fort** +2 **Ref** +5 **Will** +8 **HP** 7 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** staff +4 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +5 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning **Innate Primal Spells** DC 13 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639) **Mind if I Borrow That?** The gnome philomath designates a single item within their sight as an item of interest to their studies. They then gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) or [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) that item. They can only designate one item at a time in this way. If they use Mind if I Borrow That? to designate a new item of interest, they lose the bonus with the previous item.
name: Gnome Philomath

<title level="1" pfs="">[Bleachling Survivor](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3636)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

It is almost unheard of for gnomes to survive the horrendous sickness known as the Bleaching, but those few who do are permanently altered. Having lived through the worst disease known to their kind, bleachling survivors often emerge jaded and reckless, traveling to seek out mind-bending experiences and heady thrills that will awaken something in them again. Though their experiences may toughen them, they are not unfriendly. Most try to help others they meet during their travels who might not be as unshakable.

<column gap="tiny">

<title level="1" pfs="">[Razzle Dazzler](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3638)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The razzle dazzler is a particularly beguiling sort of entertainer who specializes in delighting with illusions and showy displays. Whether with their illusory double or some well-timed fireworks, they are exceptionally good at keeping their audiences focused on one display to distract from the secrets that make their magic possible.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/Razzle_Dazzler.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Gnome Conservationist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3639)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The natural world boasts unsurpassed beauty and countless variety, and the gnome conservationist is devoted to experiencing and protecting as many examples of this grandeur as they possibly can. These gnomes are stalwart allies to animals and plants, and they find it exceptionally easy to build an outstanding rapport with almost all creatures they encounter on their travels.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

seeds (functions as [sling bullets](/Weapons.aspx?ID=430)), _+1 [sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=430)_, trowel (functions as a [sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364))

Goblins eat almost anything. Though they'll survive on raw or little-cooked meat, some prefer searing or frying their food first—or better yet, pickling it! A goblin chef can make anything edible (at least to a goblin). **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Goblin Chef](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3640) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 186 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6) **Skills** [Cooking Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +10, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +7, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +6, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +7 **Str** +1 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Good Enough to Eat** The goblin chef can turn otherwise inedible items into meals for others. They can provide food for any number of creatures without using the [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) downtime activity as long as garbage is readily available. A non-[goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=618) who eats the goblin chef's food must attempt a DC 14 Fortitude save. On a failure, they suffer an upset stomach for 1 day; if they attempt to willingly ingest anything else during that period, they must first succeed at a DC 4 flat check or the action is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2342).
**Kitchen Specialist** For encounters involving cooking, a goblin chef is a 3rd-level challenge. **Items** cleaver (functions as [dogslicer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=405)), leather apron (functions as [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)), pickles (6) --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +6 **Will** +5 +2 circumstance bonus against [ingested](/Traits.aspx?ID=633) [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **HP** 24 **Immunities** [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** jaws +7 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d6+1 piercing **Melee** cleaver +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d4+1 slashing **Eat a Pickle** **Effect** The goblin chef draws a pickle and eats it or feeds it to an adjacent ally. The chef or ally gains 4 temporary Hit Points and ignores any penalties from [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effects or [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) for 1 round.
name: Goblin Chef

markdown:
Many goblins scavenge materials, shiny objects, and anything else they can get their hands on, but a goblin scavenger is the best at finding such useful items. More importantly, they're great at making use of them without killing themselves. Most of the time. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Goblin Scavenger](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3641) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 186 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6) **Skills** [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +12, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +9 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Items** [Big Boom Gun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=226) (10 rounds), bundle of fireworks, [dogslicer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1675), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +11 **Will** +13 **HP** 70 **Finders Keepers** **Trigger** A creature within 15 feet drops an item; **Requirements** The goblin scavenger has a hand free; **Effect** The goblin scavenger Strides up to their speed to an adjacent square and Interacts to pick up the item. The movement triggers reactions as normal, but the Interact action to pick up the item does not. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dogslicer +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d6+3 slashing **Melee** jaws +12 ([Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d8+3 piercing **Ranged** big boom gun +14 ([cobbled; fatal d12; modular B](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [or S; range 20 feet; reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+2 modular **Fireworks Barrage** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirement** The goblin scavenger has a free hand; **Effect** The goblin scavenger draws a bundle of fireworks and launches them toward a point within 60 feet, where they explode, dealing 1d10 fire damage and 1d10 sonic damage in a 10-foot burst. Every creature in the area must attempt a DC 21 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected. The goblin scavenger realizes that's because a firework fell at their feet and takes 2 fire damage when it explodes in their face.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) and [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature is also [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93). **One Person's Junk** The goblin scavenger intuitively knows how to make use of junk. When they use a weapon with the [goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=618) trait or an improvised weapon, they do an additional die of damage (already included in the Strikes above).
name: Goblin Scavenger

markdown:
There is little more terrifying than a goblin raiding party out to take from its neighbors. A swarming mix of commandos, pyros, and war chanters armed with dogslicers, horsechoppers, burning torches, and sharp teeth, this troop enjoys pillaging unprepared communities. Their song is an unsettling counterpart to the screams of their victims. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Goblin Get Gang](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3642) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 187 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10 **Str** +3 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** -1 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +14 **Will** +9 **HP** 90 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **Goblins Chant and Goblins Sing!** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 30 feet. The goblin get gang's disharmonious chorus of disturbing lyrics makes it difficult to concentrate. Any creature performing a [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) action in the area must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or the action is lost. On a critical success, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Goblins Bound and Goblins Swing!** Goblins have difficulty staying in formation, especially during combat. The goblin get gang can take a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action only if it has used at least one action to Goblins Slash and Goblins Scar! this turn or if there are no conscious enemies adjacent to it. **Goblins Burn and Goblins Char!** The troop's members throw a barrage of burning torches. Each creature in a 10-foot burst within 30 feet of the troop takes 2d8 fire damage with a DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. When the troop is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Goblins Slash and Goblins Scar!** Frequency once per round; Effect The goblin get gang uses dogslicers, horsechoppers, and burning torches to attack each enemy in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6 slashing damage and 1d6 fire damage
1d6+6 slashing damage and 1d6 fire damage
1d6+9 slashing damage and 1d6 fire damage.

creature_ability: Stab it! Stab it! Stab it!
markdown:
Big boss goblins order members of their band around and deal with any longshanks they come across—sometimes via violence or diplomacy, and sometimes by ordering another goblin to deal with them (in ways that the big boss goblin approves of if they work or claims were awful ideas they never would have tried if they don't). These goblin leaders often insist on impressive titles or sobriquets like “The Exceedingly Incendiary,” “Snake Singer,” or “Most Gluttonest,” though these can often be exaggerated by several degrees for dramatic effect or even change at the big boss goblin's whim (often to one-up a rival). **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Big Boss Goblin](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3643) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 188 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +11 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 **Items** battered crown (or other symbol of authority), [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7), _+1 [horsechopper](/Weapons.aspx?ID=409)_, [Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=77) (20 arrows) --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +11 **Will** +14 **HP** 100 **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**Not Me!** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Trigger** The big boss goblin is targeted with an attack, and a [goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=618) is adjacent to them; **Effect** The big boss goblin yanks the goblin in front of the attack to face the consequences in their stead. The big boss goblin gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their AC against the triggering attack. If it hits, the big boss goblin takes half damage, and the other goblin takes the remaining half. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _horsechopper_ +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d8+5 slashing **Melee** jaws +15 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d6+5 piercing **Ranged** shortbow +14 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing **No Fight Fair** A big boss goblin fights dirty, slashing at a foe's hamstrings. Whenever the big boss goblin hits an [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) foe, the creature takes a –5-foot status penalty to its speed (–10-foot on a critical hit) until the creature regains any amount of Hit Points. As with all penalties to Speed, this can't reduce a creature's Speed below 5 feet. **Stab it! Stab it! Stab it!** The big boss goblin picks a target they can see within 30 feet and orders any allied goblins to attack. A single [goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=618) with a lower level than the big boss goblin that is adjacent to the target can immediately use their reaction to Strike the target. In addition, until the start of the big boss goblin's next turn, their attacks against that target deal 1 additional damage dice as the big boss goblin leads them.
name: Big Boss Goblin
