markdown:
Renowned for their culinary expertise, halfling head chefs navigate the complex world of high cuisine with flavorful creations and fiery tempers. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Halfling Head Chef](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3644) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 190 **Perception** +7; (15 to smell and taste) keen eyes, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Halfling](/Languages.aspx?ID=7) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Baking Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15, [Cooking Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +7, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +6 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 **Culinary Specialist** For encounters involving cooking and taste, the head chef is a 7th-level challenge.
**Keen Eyes** The halfling gains a +2 circumstance bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) creatures within 30 feet of them. Whenever the halfling targets a creature that is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) or hidden from them, reduce the DC of the flat check to 3 for a concealed target or 9 for a hidden one. **Items** chef's hat, [Filcher's Fork](/Weapons.aspx?ID=51), [Frying Pan](/Weapons.aspx?ID=286), herbs and spices --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +7 **Will** +7 **HP** 36 **Dash of Spice** **Trigger** The head chef is targeted with a melee attack by an adjacent attacker they can see; **Effect** The head chef uses Spice Mix against the attacker. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** hot frying pan +9 ([fatal d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=597)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning plus 1d4 fire **Melee** filcher's fork +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570)), **Damage** 1d4+3 piercing **Melee** fist +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning **Ranged** filcher's fork +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d4+3 piercing **Angry Rant** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The chef shouts a flurry of insults and criticisms at either an ally or enemy within 30 feet with the following effects:
**Ally** The chef's assistant is shaken by the barrage of criticism but is determined to work faster and harder. The target becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 round but is also [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). They can use the extra action to Interact, Step, or Stride, or as part of an action or activity to prepare, cook, or serve food.
**Enemy** The target must succeed a DC 18 Will save or take 1d6 mental damage and become frightened 1 (or 2d6 mental damage and frightened 2 on a critical failure). **Spice Mix** The head chef throws a mixture of irritating spices into an adjacent creature's eyes, causing the creature to be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until it Interacts to clear its vision.
Chef's Menu A halfling head chef crafts exquisite dishes that tantalize the taste buds. Delights like honeyblossom pudding (honey, cream, and flower petals), stuffed burrow rolls (meat and vegetables wrapped in flaky pastry), sunberry glazed roast (meat marinated in sunberry sauce), and root medley gratin (layers of root vegetables and cheese) showcase their prowess.
name: Halfling Head Chef

Boisterous collectives of thrill-seeking halflings join in bill-bands to engage in spirited competitions and lively sports. They foster a sense of camaraderie among their members while leaving a trail of chaos in their wake. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Bill-Band](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3645) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 190 **Perception** +10; keen eyes **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Halfling](/Languages.aspx?ID=7) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13, [Sports Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** -1 **Cha** +2 **Keen Eyes** The halfling gains a +2 circumstance bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) creatures within 30 feet of them. Whenever the halfling targets a creature that is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) or hidden from them, reduce the DC of the flat check to 3 for a concealed target or 9 for a hidden one. --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +12 **Will** +8 +3 status vs. [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks **HP** 90 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; overwhelming scrum, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Down to Our Level** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The bill-band deliberately gets under the feet of their opponents, proving they are greater than the sum of their parts. The bill-band attempts to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) all creatures in or adjacent to their space. They roll one [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check and compare the result to the Reflex DC of each target. **Firecracker Salvo** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The bill-band launches a barrage of lit firecrackers that, upon impact, burst into light and sound. Each creature in a 10-foot burst within 60 feet takes 1d12 sonic damage with a DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that fails its save is also [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round. When the bill-band is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Overwhelming Scrum** The bill-band swarms in and around other creatures. They can move into other creatures' spaces, and other creatures can move into their spaces. The bill-band's spaces are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) to other creatures. **Stick It to 'Em!** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The bill-band swings barely coordinated fists and feet at each enemy in their space and in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6 bludgeoning damage
2d6+4 bludgeoning damage
3d6+6 bludgeoning damage.
name: Bill-Band

Halfling smugglers are sought out for their ability to deftly navigate the shadowy underworld to move illicit goods and information. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Halfling Smuggler](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3646) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 191 **Perception** +13; keen eyes **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Halfling](/Languages.aspx?ID=7) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +16, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 **Grease Some Palms** A smuggler is adept at navigating official channels and makes network contacts in order to keep their goods moving. They gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) and [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) with members of the local bureaucracy.
**Keen Eyes** The halfling gains a +2 circumstance bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) creatures within 30 feet of them. Whenever the halfling targets a creature that is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) or hidden from them, reduce the DC of the flat check to 3 for a concealed target or 9 for a hidden one. **Items** [Arsenic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=104), [Disguise Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), [lesser elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91), _+1 [filcher's fork](/Weapons.aspx?ID=407)_, [fine clothes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72), [lesser smoke ball](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3360), [Thieves' Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758) --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +16 **Will** +13 **HP** 95 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _filcher's fork_ +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d4+9 piercing **Melee** fist +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+9 bludgeoning **Ranged** _filcher's fork_ +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d4+9 piercing **Ranged** sling +16 ([Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning **Distracting Escape** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Smugglers succeed by making a move only after they've diverted others' attention. The smuggler [Creates a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387). If the smuggler became hidden to at least one creature, the smuggler can then [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Hidden Pockets** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The smuggler Interacts to draw an item of light Bulk concealed in one of their hidden pockets. The pockets can store up to four objects of light Bulk. For most smugglers, these items are [arsenic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3322), a [lesser elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308), a [lesser smoke ball](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3360), and a [thieves' toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758). The smuggler can refill the pockets over the course of 1 minute. **Sneak Attack** The smuggler deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.
name: Halfling Smuggler

markdown:
Yarnspinners weave captivating tales that entertain, educate, and preserve the rich heritage of the halfling people across generations. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Halfling Yarnspinner](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3647) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 192 **Perception** +14; keen eyes **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Halfling](/Languages.aspx?ID=7) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [History Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +19, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +17, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +15 **Str** -1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Keen Eyes** The halfling gains a +2 circumstance bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) creatures within 30 feet of them. Whenever the halfling targets a creature that is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) or hidden from them, reduce the DC of the flat check to 3 for a concealed target or 9 for a hidden one.
**Tale Specialist** For encounters involving storytelling, local history, or lore, the yarnspinner is a 10th-level challenge. **Items** book of fables, [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6), _+1 [halfling sling staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=438)_ (20 bullets), _+1 [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398)_ --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +15 **Will** +18 **HP** 110 **Guidance Through Tales** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** An ally the yarnspinner can see fails a skill check; **Effect** The yarnspinner offers a brief reminder about a legendary hero, granting their ally a +2 circumstance bonus to the triggering skill check, potentially turning the failure into a success. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _shortsword_ +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing plus resonant weapons **Melee** fist +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning **Ranged** _halfling sling staff_ +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d10+3 bludgeoning plus resonant weapons **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 26, attack +18 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **1st** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Laughing Fit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653), [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1678) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555), [Ring of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656), [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1678) (4 slots) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Honeyed Words](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (3 slots) **Mesmerizing Tale** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The yarnspinner weaves a long-winded but captivating narrative that enchants those nearby. Any creature that's in a 20-foot emanation or starts its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 24 Will save. The Mesmerizing Tale lasts until the end of the yarnspinner's next turn, but can be Sustained. The first time the yarnspinner Sustains the aura on subsequent rounds, the aura expands by 10 feet, to a maximum of 60 feet.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, and is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with the yarnspinner until the start of its next turn, and must spend all its actions to move closer to the yarnspinner and listen to the tale. **Resonant Weapons** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) If the yarnspinner's Mesmerizing Tale aura is active or they have cast a spell within the last round, their Strikes with magic weapons deal an additional 2d10 sonic damage.
name: Halfling Yarnspinner

<title level="1" pfs="">[Hobgoblin Spellbreaker](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3648)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Largely due to an ancestral grudge against all things [elven](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=414), many hobgoblins have an inherent distrust for magic, particularly the “elf magic” of the arcane tradition. Most hobgoblin armies maintain a contingent of special “spellbreaker” forces trained to assassinate high-value spellcasting targets prior to military engagement or quickly identify and terminate enemy battle mages.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Hobgoblin Spellbreaker](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3648)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Hobgoblin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=757" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 194

**Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), arcane magic sense ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +9, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +1

**Int** +3

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Arcane Magic Sense** The hobgoblin spellbreaker can detect the source of any [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) magic within range as an [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) sense.

**Items**
[Breaching Pike](/Weapons.aspx?ID=274), [Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (10 bolts), [Scale Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=8), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +6 

**Will** +9 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 50

</row>

**Spellbreaking Reactive Strike** <actions string="Reaction" /> As [Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48), but if it was triggered by a creature casting an [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) spell, the target must succeed at a DC 11 flat check or the spell is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312). If the Strike was a critical hit, the spell is disrupted automatically.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
breaching pike +12 ([Razing](/Traits.aspx?ID=784), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
crossbow +10 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8+3 piercing

**Shatter Spell** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The hobgoblin spellbreaker attempts a melee Strike against a creature under the effects of a beneficial [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) spell. If the Strike hits and deals damage, the hobgoblin spellbreaker can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) a single arcane spell or arcane magical effect on the target (counteract rank 2, counteract modifier +10). If it fails, the hobgoblin spellbreaker can't attempt to counteract the same effect for 1 hour.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-430" />
name: Hobgoblin Spellbreaker

A hobgoblin battalion is usually composed of multiple soldiers and archers commanded by a single captain, although every battalion also has its own internal hierarchy that clearly defines the chain of command should the current captain fall in battle. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Hobgoblin Battalion](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3649) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 194 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +0 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +12 **Will** +14 **HP** 90 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 8 **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Coordinated Strikes** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The battalion thrusts their spears at each enemy in a 5-foot emanation with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+2 slashing damage
2d6+5 slashing damage
3d12+7 slashing damage **Focused Volley** The hobgoblin battalion's archers draw or reload their crossbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a 10-foot burst within 120 feet that deals 2d8 piercing damage with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. When the hobgoblin battalion is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area is reduced to a 5-foot burst. **Perfect Formation** The battalion raises a perfect guard against explosions. It gains a +2 item bonus to AC and a +2 status bonus to Reflex saves until the start of its next turn.
name: Hobgoblin Battalion

<title level="1" pfs="">[Hobgoblin Vanguard](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3650)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Though there are times for precision and discipline, every hobgoblin general also understands the value of demoralizing the enemy with a show of overwhelming force. No military unit is better suited to this purpose than the vanguard, a heavily armed and armored elite unit that inspires their fellow soldiers to action while breaking the enemy's lines and morale with terrifying explosive weapons.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

If the target creature was reduced to 0 Hit Points by the triggering Strike, the hobgoblin vanguard can instead attempt to Demoralize all opponents within 30 feet, rolling once and comparing the result to each target's Will DC.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Bone Scavenger](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3651)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Kholos have a strong connection to their ancestors, often using their bones as art and weapons to further honor them. Bone scavengers are sent to the fields after a battle to collect the bones of their fellow allies. They often eschew the pack hunting techniques of other kholos, spreading out among their foes when encountered in groups.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 14

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/Bone_Scavenger.webp" />
</column>

**Will** +3

All adjacent creatures take 2d4 piercing damage (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

</column>

---

**Melee**

**Damage** 1d4+2 piercing

**Rugged Travel**   A kholo ignores the first square of [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) they move into each time they Step or Stride.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Ant Kholos</title>

The bone scavenger belongs to the ant kholo heritage, smaller and with larger ears than other kholos. You can change this NPC to a different kholo heritage by making it Medium, and you can conversely change a different kholo NPC to an ant kholo by making their size Small.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Kholo Pragmatist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3652)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Due to their aggressive lifestyle, kholo warriors rarely live to an old age. When a kholo pragmatist enters a fight, they would prefer to see another battle, so they use any possible advantage they can get to survive. They keep foes at a distance with their longspears have developed techniques to better block attacks with the weapon. If the tide of battle turns against them, kholo pragmatists will run or surrender if it's the most practical option.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

---

<column gap="tiny">

---

**Pragmatic Aid** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Requirements** The kholo pragmatist is adjacent to a foe; **Effect** The kholo pragmatist sets up an advantageous avenue of attack for an ally within 10 feet of the same foe and then Steps away from that foe. The foe is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the kholo pragmatist's ally's next attack.

**Rugged Travel**   A kholo ignores the first square of [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) they move into each time they Step or Stride.

**Spear Parry** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The kholo pragmatist is wielding a longspear; **Effect** The kholo pragmatist positions their spear defensively, gaining a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of their next turn.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Kholo Outrider](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3653)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Many kholo packs are semi-nomadic, staying in one place until they're pushed out by others, or the local resources begin to grow sparse. Before moving the pack elsewhere, the leaders send out a single kholo to blaze a trail and ensure a safe route to their next destination. These outriders are masters of the wilderness and fight better when they're alone.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

<title level="1" pfs="">[Kobold Trapper](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3654)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Kobolds are skillful artisans, always inventing new traps and snares to defend their territory and ambush enemies. Kobold trappers enjoy showing off their crafting prowess on the battlefield.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Kobold_Trapper.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Kobold Trapper](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3654)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Kobold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=759" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 198

**Perception** +7; (9 to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) for [traps](/Traits.aspx?ID=715)) [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +5, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +3

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Booby-Trapped** A kobold trapper protects items in their backpack with a booby trap. This booby trap requires a successful DC 18 Perception check to notice, and two successful DC 15 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks to disable. Accessing the backpack without disabling the trap destroys its contents, and splinters explode in a 10-foot burst centered on the backpack, dealing 3d6 piercing damage (DC 15 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

**Items**
[Backpack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4), [Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (20 bolts), formula book (containing formulas for three 1st- or 2nd-level [snares](/Traits.aspx?ID=809)), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Light Hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=384)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +8 

+1 circumstance to all defenses vs. [traps](/Traits.aspx?ID=715)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 32

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
light hammer +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
crossbow +9 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
light hammer +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 bludgeoning

**Construct Trap** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The kobold trapper creates a rudimentary trap on a surface in an adjacent square. The trap activates the next time a creature moves adjacent to it. The creature takes 2d6 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (determined by the trapper when the trap is constructed) with a DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. On a failure, the creature also takes a –5 status penalty to all Speeds for 1 minute. The trap is destroyed when activated or after 8 hours, whichever comes first. A trapper typically carries enough raw materials to make six traps each day.

</column>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Kobold egg guardians are some of the best warriors in a tribe, tasked with protecting the next generation. They pledge to give their lives to protect the tribe's eggs, though not before exhausting all their tricks.

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +0

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +3

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +6 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 48

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
longspear +12 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d8+5 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+5 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
crossbow +12 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8+2 piercing

**Immobilizing Thrust** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The kobold egg guardian makes a longspear Strike. If the Strike hits, the target must attempt a DC 20 Reflex save. On a failure, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until the kobold egg guardian moves, attacks with the longspear, or is no longer wielding the longspear.

**Luring Retreat** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The kobold egg guardian screams and Strides up to their Speed. Each enemy who sees or hears the kobold egg guardian must succeed at a DC 17 Will save or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by the egg guardian for 1 round. On the creature's turn, it must use at least 1 action (or 2 actions on a critical failure) to move closer to the kobold egg guardian (while avoiding obvious dangers). Regardless of the result of the save, targets are then immune to Luring Retreat for 24 hours.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Root Leshy Groundskeeper](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3656)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hardy root leshies have the strength and stamina to dig furrows for planting seeds over long hours. Though they're humble even among leshies, they can be extremely stubborn even against powerful adversaries.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Root_Leshy_Groundskeeper.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature -1">[Root Leshy Groundskeeper](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3656)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Leshy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=639" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=668" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 200

**Perception** +5; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) (root vegetables only)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +5, [Labor Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +2, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +4, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +0

**Con** +3

**Int** -1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
shovel (functions as [pick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=389))

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 14 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +2 

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 9

</row>

**Verdant Burst** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) When the root leshy groundskeeper dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from their body, restoring 1d4 Hit Points to each plant creature in a 30-foot emanation. This area immediately fills with roots and vines, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). If the terrain is not a viable environment for these plants, they wither after 24 hours.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shovel +5 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597)),
**Damage** 1d6+3 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +5 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 13
- **Constant (3rd)**
[Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) (root vegetables only)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The root leshy groundskeeper transforms into a Small root vegetable. This ability otherwise uses the effects of [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618).

**Root in Place** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The root leshy groundskeeper roots themself into the ground, reducing their Speed to 0 and granting them a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and 2 temporary Hit Points until the start of their next turn.

</column>

markdown:
A tumbleweed leshy's ability to move quickly across long distances with ease allows them to act as messengers and go-betweens. [Druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=6) often entrust these couriers with delivering important messages to their home druid circles or other druids they're acquainted with. Some even induct the couriers into the circle to teach them [Wildsong](/Languages.aspx?ID=116) for secure communication and basic magic for self-defense. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Tumbleweed Leshy Courier](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3657) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 200 **Perception** +12; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) (tumbleweeds and scrubland brush only) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +10, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **Tumbling Traveler** The tumbleweed leshy courier gains a +10-foot circumstance bonus to Speed while traveling during exploration mode. **Items** [Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (20 bolts), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +12 **Will** +9 **HP** 35 **Spiny Burst** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) When the tumbleweed leshy courier dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from their body, restoring 2d8 Hit Points to each plant creature in a 30-foot emanation. This area immediately fills with brambles and thistles, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). Any creature that moves through the area takes 1 piercing damage per square traversed. If the terrain is not a viable environment for these plants, they wither after 24 hours.
**Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** The tumbleweed leshy courier is targeted with an attack by an attacker they can see; **Effect** The leshy gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** dagger +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+5 piercing **Melee** fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+5 bludgeoning **Ranged** crossbow +12 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 17 - **Constant (3rd)** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) (tumbleweeds and scrubland brush only) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The tumbleweed leshy courier transforms into a Small tumbleweed. This ability otherwise uses the effects of [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618). Additionally, when the leshy uses their Change Shape ability, they still have a Speed of 10 feet for the purpose of travel during exploration mode.

markdown:
A thick forest or flourishing farm sometimes sees an explosion of primal magic leading to the creation of a multitude of leshies. When still young, these spirits might band together with their crop-mates for protection or to achieve a mutual goal. Used to growing in rows, many corn leshies can form a sizable legion. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Corn Leshy Throng](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3658) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 201 **Perception** +10; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) (corn only) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +12 **Str** +2 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +13 **Will** +10 **HP** 54 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, splash damage 5 **Encircling Maze** A corn leshy throng is arranged in rows of stalks to envelop foes, stretching upward to block their vision. It can move into other creatures' spaces, and other creatures can move into its squares.
When a Medium or smaller creature attempts to enter any of the corn leshy throng's spaces, it must attempt a DC 20 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) check. If the creature fails, it gets turned around—all the throng's squares are [greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for it until the end of this turn. A creature needs to attempt this check only the first time in a round it attempts to enter one of the throng's squares.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)**
**Verdant Burst** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) When the corn leshy throng dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from their body, restoring 3d8 Hit Points to each plant creature in a 30-foot emanation. This area immediately fills with stalks of corn, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). If the terrain is not a viable environment for these plants, they wither after 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Boxing Ears** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The corn leshy throng lashes out with hardened ears of corn to attack each enemy in its space and in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6 bludgeoning damage
2d6+4 bludgeoning damage
2d6+8 bludgeoning damage **Kernel Barrage** The throng's members fling a bombardment of corn kernels. Each creature in a 30-foot cone takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. When the throng is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 15-foot cone.
name: Corn Leshy Throng

<title level="1" pfs="">[Gourd Leshy Witch](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3659)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Some spooky leshies learn the ways of occult spellcasting from strange spirits of nature that lurk just out of sight or under cover of darkness. These witches treat their brooms as their familiars, imbuing the wood and straw with a sliver of sentience.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Gourd_Leshy_Witch.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Gourd Leshy Witch](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3659)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Leshy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=639" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=668" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 202

**Perception** +12; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) (gourds only)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +14, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +1

**Int** +4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Items**
_+1 broom_ (functions as a [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 22 

**Fort** +11 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 80

</row>

**Verdant Burst** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) When the gourd leshy witch dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from their body, restoring 4d8 Hit Points to each plant creature in a 30-foot emanation. This area immediately sprouts gourds, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). If the terrain is not a viable environment for these plants, they wither after 24 hours.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_broom_ +13 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand 1d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning plus 1d6 void

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 piercing plus 1d6 void

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning plus 1d6 void

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 piercing plus 1d6 void

**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 24, attack +16
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
- **1st**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (×2), [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566)
- **2nd**
[Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=1623)
- **3rd**
[Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677), [Vampiric Feast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736)

**Witch Hex Spells** DC 24, 1 Focus Point
- **3rd**
[Wilding Word](/Spells.aspx?ID=1893)

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 24
- **Constant (3rd)**
[Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) (gourds only)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [primal The gourd leshy witch transforms into a Small gourd. This ability otherwise uses the effects of [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618).](/Traits.aspx?ID=668)) 

**Short Flight** <actions string="Single Action to Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The gourd leshy witch is wielding a broom; **Effect** The gourd leshy hops on their broom, which briefly takes flight. The witch [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) 20 feet (or 40 feet if they spend 2 actions), though they must end this movement on solid ground or fall at the end of their turn.

**Sweeping Spell** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Requirements** The gourd leshy witch is wielding their broom; **Effect** If the next action the gourd leshy witch uses is to cast a non-[cantrip](/Traits.aspx?ID=550) spell that deals damage to a single target, the witch's broom flies out and attempts to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) that creature with an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) modifier of +16. On a critical success, the target is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The broom immediately returns to the gourd leshy witch's hand.

</column>

name: Gourd Leshy Witch

Iruxi tidewater guards are capable fighters skilled at amphibious attacks and overpowering vessels along any shore. Because lizardfolk settlements are typically constructed partially underwater and partially above, they have need of defenders who can guard from attacks in both environments. The special spaulders tidewater guards wear set them apart from other lizardfolk warriors. These protect their shoulders from cutlass and axe strikes, and might be constructed of dragon scales, turtle or giant chiton shells, lacquered wood, or even sea urchin tests. The recipe for the special resin iruxi armorers apply to harden these spaulders is a closely guarded secret, and each iruxi community's supply of the resin is small, precious, and closely guarded. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Tidewater Guard](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3660) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 204 **Perception** +10 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Iruxi](/Languages.aspx?ID=32) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** -1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Deep Breath** A tidewater guard can [hold their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=2436) for 20 minutes.
**Tethered Tridents** The tidewater guard's tridents are specially prepared to be aquadynamic and tethered by ropes. They have the [tethered](/Traits.aspx?ID=804) trait, meaning that a wielder who has a free hand can Interact to pull the weapon back into their grasp after they have thrown it as a ranged attack or after it has been disarmed (unless it is being held by another creature). **Items** [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5), [trident with 50 feet of line](/Weapons.aspx?ID=45) (2) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +11 **Will** +10 **HP** 60 **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** trident +14 ([Tethered](/Traits.aspx?ID=804)), **Damage** 1d8+7 piercing **Melee** claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d6+7 piercing **Ranged** trident +13 ([Tethered](/Traits.aspx?ID=804), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d8+7 piercing **Reel In** The tidewater guard makes a ranged Strike with their trident. If the Strike hits, the guard can haul on the attached line, [moving](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) the target up to 30 feet in a straight line toward the iruxi. **Terrain Advantage** Non-[lizardfolk](/Traits.aspx?ID=760) creatures that are in [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) or are in water and lack a swim Speed are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the tidewater guard.
name: Tidewater Guard

Iruxi masked mummers bring lizardfolk myths to life, donning elaborate costumes and bone masks to enact stories featuring their ancestors, the gods, legendary dragons and dinosaurs, and nature spirits of all kinds. The most talented mummers claim their ritual dances invite these spirits inside them, suffusing their bodies with starlight and lending them primal power uniquely tied to the luminaries their masks represent. This magic can be seen even by those without magical ability, as they can see glowing motes shaped like distant stars seeming to float under the skin of the mummer. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Iruxi Masked Mummer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3661) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 205 **Perception** +18 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Iruxi](/Languages.aspx?ID=32), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +19, [Iruxi Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +20, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +18, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +20, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +18 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Deep Breath** A masked mummer can [hold their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=2436) for 20 minutes. **Items** [ceremonial skull mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1397), _+1 striking [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086)_, mummer's costume, [lesser maestro instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3026) (hand drum) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +19 **Will** +18 **HP** 155 --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** tail +20 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning **Melee** claws +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+8 slashing **Ranged** spectral roar +19 ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)), **Damage** 3d10 sonic **Don Mask** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The mummer dons a ceremonial skull mask, calling an ancestral spirit to come to their aid. The spirit answers with a spell the mummer can cast as a primal innate spell (5th rank, DC 27) as part of the Don Mask activity. The mummer also gains a primal boon that lasts for 1 minute. After the minute is over or the mask is removed, the spell and boon end if either is still active, and the mummer is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73). The most common legendary spirits the masks can invoke are:
  • **Fiery Akkarok** (tyrannosaurus mask); **Spell** [_blazing bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450) (3-action version); **Boon** The mummer's melee Strikes deal an additional 2d6 fire damage.
  • **Hazi Zephyr-Borne** (griffon or iruxi mask); **Spell** [_wall of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1753); **Boon** The mummer gains a fly Speed of 20 feet.
  • **King of Storms** (roc or horned dragon mask); Spell [_howling blizzard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559); **Boon** The mummer gains the [Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48) reaction that can be used only with their tail.
  • **Nessek, the Wave Dancer** (mosasaur mask); **Spell** [_slither_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1676); **Boon** The mummer's claw Strikes deal an additional 2d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
  • **Zalok, Who was Called to Black Harbor** (naga or spinosaurus mask); **Spell** [_hydraulic torrent_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562); **Boon** The mummer gains a +10-foot status bonus to their land Speed and swim Speed.
**Starry Presence** ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 30 feet. With a quick dance, the iruxi masked mummer surrounds themself with a starlight image resembling a figure from legend. The image lasts until the start of the mummer's next turn. An enemy that enters or starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 25 Will save or become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the start of its next turn.
name: Iruxi Masked Mummer

<title level="1" pfs="">[Orc Agriculturalist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3662)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In the severe lands orcs occupy, there are no lush fields blooming with crops. An orc farmer must be tough and just as adept at foraging as planting and harvesting.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Orc Agriculturalist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3662)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Orc" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=666" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 206

**Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Orcish](/Languages.aspx?ID=9)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +5, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +3, [Farming Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Farming Specialist** For encounters involving farming, harvesting, or identifying plants, the agriculturalist is a 5th-level challenge.

**Items**
[Blowgun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=66) (20 darts), pitchfork (as [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=361)), poisonous herb (5), [Sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=9)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 14 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +6 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 25

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pitchfork +7 ([Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d8+2 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sickle +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
blowgun +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1 piercing

**Herbal Poison** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The agriculturalist quickly turns some of their supplies of poisonous herbs into an herbal poison, then applies it to a melee weapon or piece of ammunition in their possession. The next successful attack with a weapon poisoned this way deals an additional 1d6 poison damage. The applied poison fades after its damage is applied to an attack or 1 minute passes, whichever happens first.

**Poison Detector** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The orc agriculturalist attempts a [Farming Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) check to determine whether an object is [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) or has been poisoned. The DC is the poison's DC (if any), or the standard DC of the poison's level. On a critical success, they also learn the number and types of poison involved.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-454" />
name: Orc Agriculturalist

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Gamekeepers live on the outskirts of the hold, usually remaining solitary and tending to animals they've captured. Every part of a trapped animal can be useful for making supplies or trading.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Orc Gamekeeper](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3663)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Orc" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=666" />
</traits>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Lorekeepers are meticulous keepers of the colorful and often misunderstood history of orcs and dromaar. They're jovial wanderers, telling their stories to anyone who will listen.

[Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516), [Heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555) (2 slots)

**a**

markdown:
Orc warriors search for more effective weapons and tactics—most physical, but some magical. As they fought back against the undead on their borders, they formed bands of skullcrushers to crush hordes of skeletons. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Orc Skullcrushers](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3665) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 207 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Orcish](/Languages.aspx?ID=9) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +13 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +16 **Will** +13 **HP** 120 (4 segments) **Resistances** void 8 **Weaknesses** area damage 8, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 8 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Chant of Dominance** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Effect** Orc war drummers lead the other skullcrushers in a holy chant extolling their superiority in battle. Any creature damaged by the skullcrushers this turn also takes 1d6 [persistent spirit damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). **Crush Skulls** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The skullcrushers smash their mauls and clubs against each enemy in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d12 bludgeoning damage
1d12+8 bludgeoning damage
2d12+8 bludgeoning damage **Sacred Salvo** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) The skullcrushers fling a fusillade of sling bullets enchanted with life energy intended to destroy undead. This barrage is a 10-foot burst within 50 feet that deals 3d6 bludgeoning damage plus 1d6 vitality damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), with a DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. When the troop is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst.
name: Orc Skullcrushers

markdown:
While the sword and shield are reliable and proven in battle, the veteran master is the weapon. They have been hardened by decades of fighting, but they still manage to find peace within themselves to gain a physical advantage. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Orc Veteran Master](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3666) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 208 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Orcish](/Languages.aspx?ID=9) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +20 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372)_, _+1 [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=47)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=434)_ (20 arrows) --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +20 **Will** +18 **HP** 175 **Fly Through Battle** The veteran master gains an additional reaction each round that can be used only to make a Reactive Pursuit.
**Reactive Pursuit** **Trigger** An enemy within reach attempts to move away; **Effect** The veteran master Strides up to their Speed, following the enemy and keeping it in reach throughout its movement until it stops moving or the master has moved their full Speed.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _bo staff_ +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 2d8+13 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 2d4+13 bludgeoning **Ranged** _composite longbow_ +23 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 2d8+10 piercing **Staff Swipe** The veteran master extends their reach to smash multiple creatures with their bo. They attempt a bo staff Strike against each enemy in a 15-foot cone. This counts as two attacks toward their multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks. **Reshape the Battle** The veteran master attempts a bo staff Strike. If it hits a creature of the master's size or smaller, the master can automatically [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379) it to any space within the bo staff's reach.
name: Orc Veteran Master

<title level="1" pfs="">[Tunnel Viper](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3667)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

There is no better way for a ysoki to distinguish themself than to defend the warren, with many proving themselves by venturing out and challenging monsters they come across. Those who return triumphant from such tests find themselves among the ranks of ysoki warriors, able to wear their accomplishments as a badge of pride. They often venture out again once established and experienced to attain more notoriety as warriors.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

</column>

**Will** +5

**Damage** 1d10+3 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
arbalest +8 ([Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [range increment 110 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d10 piercing

**Running Reload** <actions string="Single Action" />  The tunnel viper Strides, Steps, or [Sneaks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), then Interacts to reload.

**Swarming**   A tunnel viper can end their movement in the same square as an ally that also has this ability. Only two such creatures can share the same space.

**Tunnel Fighter**   The tunnel viper deals an additional 1d6 precision damage to creatures that are [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372) or in [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) due to narrow spaces.

**Tunnel Travel**   Narrow spaces aren't [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for the tunnel viper, and the viper can [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372) at 5 feet per round (or 10 feet on a critical success).

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Swarm Voice](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3668)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The swarm voice is the secular leader of a ysoki warren or one family in a larger warren. If there is a dispute, the swarm voice resolves it. If there is a negotiation, they orchestrate it. If war is about to break out, they declare it. The swarm voice is the welcoming hand and the iron fist of their colony.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

crossbow +10 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8+3 piercing

**Advise Swarm** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The swarm voice issues orders to move. Each ratfolk from the same warren in a 15-foot emanation can spend a reaction to Step, Stride, or [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307).

**Chittering Terror** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The swarm voice chitters, creating a terrifying din, and encourages their allies to join in. Each enemy within 30 feet must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or be [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (or frightened 2 on a critical failure). An enemy takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its save if it's adjacent to one or more [ratfolk](/Traits.aspx?ID=769) allied with the swarm voice. Regardless of the result of a creature's save, it's then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

**Swarming**   A ysoki can end their movement in the same square as an ally that also has this ability. Only two such creatures can share the same space.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Bone Mother](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3669)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The bone mother—a warren's oracle—cuts an imposing figure. Bone mothers can be any gender despite the name, wearing the skull of a giant rat and covering their clothing in dangling bones. When a member of the warren dies, they gift a bone (usually a finger bone) to the oracle, who exists as both a physical repository of those who came before and a living history of their warren.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/Bone_Mother.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Gambling Companion](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3670)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A reputation for protecting against bad luck, combined with skill and knowledge, establish these tengu as ideal gambling companions. Their role includes providing local knowledge of establishments as well as the various games available. Refined skills of observation and an ability to read other players increases the value of their services. The example provided is for a companion who frequents the finer establishments. For some areas, a patron would look for a companion with greater fighting skills to also fill the role of bodyguard.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Gambling_Companion.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Tengu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=776" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 212

**Perception** +12; (14 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Tengu](/Languages.aspx?ID=47); plus three others

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +11, [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +9, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** +2

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Social Specialist** For social encounters involving gaming or gambling, the gambling companion is a 5th-level challenge.

**Items**
cards, [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) (3), dice

</column>

**Will** +9

**Gamer's Guidance** ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) When the gambling companion successfully [Aids](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) a skill check related to games or gambling, the ally rolls twice and takes the higher result instead of gaining the usual bonus.

</column>

---

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +10 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),

<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 piercing

**Distracting Trick** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The gambling companion is wielding cards or dice; **Effect** The gambling companion performs a quick trick with the cards or dice to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390), then makes a beak Strike against the same target. If the Feint succeeds, the Strike deals an additional 1d6 precision damage.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Jinx Eater](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3671)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Whether kidnapped, conscripted, or having entered voluntary service, a tengu in the role of jinx eater on a ship's crew is tasked with keeping the crew free of misfortune. Those with the necessary skill to do so often achieve a respected and privileged position on board.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Jinx Eater](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3671)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Tengu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=776" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 212

**Perception** +12; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Tengu](/Languages.aspx?ID=47); plus two others

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +10, [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +1

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Items**
bottle, [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Tengu Gale Blade](/Weapons.aspx?ID=479)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 65

</row>

**Eat Fortune** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet uses a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect; **Effect** The tengu negates the attempt to manipulate fate and fortune. Eat Fortune gains the opposing trait, and the triggering effect is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2342).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tengu gale blade +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +13 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 piercing

**Jinxed Call** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The jinx eater gives an eerie croak. Each non-[tengu](/Traits.aspx?ID=776) in a 30-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 21 Will save or be [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) for 1 round (or 1 minute on a critical failure). Regardless of the results, each creature is then temporarily immune to Jinxed Call for 1 minute.

**Sneak Attack**   The jinx eater deals 1d6 extra precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Tengu Bladesmith](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3672)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

With the tengu diaspora spreading across much of Golarion, their knowledge and tradition of blade crafting has been passed through the generations to those who show interest and aptitude. Many tengu bladesmiths have experience as warriors; others learn their sword craft to improve their knowledge and understanding of the weapons they produce.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Tengu Bladesmith](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3672)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Tengu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=776" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 213

**Perception** +14; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Tengu](/Languages.aspx?ID=47); plus two others

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Tengu Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** +1

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Items**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) [wakizashi](/Weapons.aspx?ID=419), _+1 [katana](/Weapons.aspx?ID=411)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +16 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 100

</row>

**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_katana_ +17 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=718), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d6+7 slashing plus 1d4 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
cold iron wakizashi +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+7 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +15,
**Damage** 1d6+7 piercing

**Feinting Failure** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The tengu bladesmith's previous action this turn was a Strike that failed or critically failed; **Effect** The tengu bladesmith Strikes the same target, who is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against this attack. On a hit, the bladesmith deals 1d6 additional precision damage.

**Swirling Blade** <actions string="Single Action" />  The tengu bladesmith Interacts to draw a weapon in the [sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15) group, then attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) a weapon held by a foe within reach. The weapon the tengu bladesmith draws gains the [disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) trait for this attempt.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-476" />
name: Tengu Bladesmith
